'''Migukurumitama Shrine''' (美具久留御魂神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Tondabayashi|ja|富田林市}}, [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is an {{ill|historic shrine|ja|式内社}} and was formerly ranked as a {{ill|gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. The shrine is also selected as one of the {{ill|100 Green Selections of Osaka|ja|大阪みどりの百選}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/midorikikaku/toshiryokka/hyakusenn.html|title=Osaka 100 Green Selections|publisher=Osaka Prefecture|accessdate=2016-12-23}}</ref> It is also referred to as the "Shimono Suibun Shrine" (下水分社) and, along with {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine|ja|建水分神社}} (the Kamisuibun Shrine in {{ill|Chihayaakasaka|ja|千早赤阪村}}) and {{ill|Nishikori Shrine|ja|錦織神社}} (in Tondabayashi), it is one of the "Three Mikumari Shrines of {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}."
== Enshrined Deities == * '''Primary Deity:''' Migukurumitama no Ōkami (美具久留御魂大神) – The {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒魂・和魂}} (rough spirit) of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}}. It is believed to represent a water deity, deriving its name from "Mikukurumitama" (Water Swimming Spirit). * '''Additional Deities:''' * {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Amemikumari no Kami|ja|天水分神}} (Heavenly Mikumari Deity) * {{ill|Mizuhanome|lt=Mizuhanome no Mikoto|ja|弥都波迺売命}} * {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Kuni no Mikumari no Kami|ja|国水分神}} * {{ill|Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseribime no Mikoto|ja|須勢理比売命}} * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶比売神}}
== History == === Foundation === According to tradition, the shrine was founded in the 10th year of Emperor Sujin's reign (88 BCE). A giant serpent frequently appeared in the area, leading the emperor to identify it as the aramitama of Ōkuninushi. He dispatched {{ill|Izumo Furune|ja|出雲振根}} to enshrine the deity, borrowing sacred objects from {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|杵築大社}}.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Place Name Dictionary|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1986}}</ref>
In Emperor Sujin's 62nd year, an oracle declared that "Mikukurumitama" should be the deity's name, and the shrine was established to house this deity alongside four others.
=== Historical Records === The shrine may correspond to the "Kawachi Province Wani Shrine" mentioned in 850 CE, where the deity's rank was raised to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}}. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, it also served as a clan shrine for the {{ill|Kusunoki family|ja|楠木氏}}, who venerated the shrine as their ancestral deity.
The shrine was burned down during the {{ill|Siege of Akasaka|ja|赤坂城の戦い}} in 1331 but was later rebuilt under the support of {{ill|Kusunoki Masashige|ja|楠木正成}}. In 1585, it was again destroyed during the {{ill|Kishū Campaign|ja|紀州征伐}} by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}}. The current main shrine was reconstructed in 1660.
The shrine was designated as a gōsha in 1872 during the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}.
== Shrine Grounds == The shrine grounds include the following structures: * '''Main Shrine (Honden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Upper Worship Hall (Kami-haiden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Lower Worship Hall (Shimo-haiden):''' * '''Treasure Hall (Saigu-kura):''' * '''Gates and Torii:''' * '''Miyajinja Uramayama Kofun Group:''' Includes a keyhole-shaped kofun (58 m long) believed to be from the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Uramayama Kofun Explanation Board|publisher=Educational Board of Tondabayashi|accessdate=2020-08-28}}</ref>
== Subsidiary Shrines == * {{ill|Kumano Takahira Shrine|ja|熊野貴平神社}} * {{ill|Nagi Shrine|ja|南木神社}} – Dedicated to the Kusunoki family. * {{ill|Kuni no Ichi Shrine|ja|皇大神社}}
== Annual Festivals == The shrine's annual festival is held on the third Friday of October.

'''Migukurumitama Shrine''' (美具久留御魂神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Tondabayashi|ja|富田林市}}, [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is an {{ill|historic shrine|ja|式内社}} and was formerly ranked as a {{ill|gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. The shrine is also selected as one of the {{ill|100 Green Selections of Osaka|ja|大阪みどりの百選}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/midorikikaku/toshiryokka/hyakusenn.html|title=Osaka 100 Green Selections|publisher=Osaka Prefecture|accessdate=2016-12-23}}</ref> It is also referred to as the "Shimono Suibun Shrine" (下水分社) and, along with {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine|ja|建水分神社}} (the Kamisuibun Shrine in {{ill|Chihayaakasaka|ja|千早赤阪村}}) and {{ill|Nishikori Shrine|ja|錦織神社}} (in Tondabayashi), it is one of the "Three Mikumari Shrines of {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}."
== Enshrined Deities == * '''Primary Deity:''' Migukurumitama no Ōkami (美具久留御魂大神) – The {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒魂・和魂}} (rough spirit) of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}}. It is believed to represent a water deity, deriving its name from "Mikukurumitama" (Water Swimming Spirit). * '''Additional Deities:''' * {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Amemikumari no Kami|ja|天水分神}} (Heavenly Mikumari Deity) * {{ill|Mizuhanome|lt=Mizuhanome no Mikoto|ja|弥都波迺売命}} * {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Kuni no Mikumari no Kami|ja|国水分神}} * {{ill|Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseribime no Mikoto|ja|須勢理比売命}} * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶比売神}}
== History == === Foundation === According to tradition, the shrine was founded in the 10th year of Emperor Sujin's reign (88 BCE). A giant serpent frequently appeared in the area, leading the emperor to identify it as the aramitama of Ōkuninushi. He dispatched {{ill|Izumo Furune|ja|出雲振根}} to enshrine the deity, borrowing sacred objects from {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|杵築大社}}.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Place Name Dictionary|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1986}}</ref>
In Emperor Sujin's 62nd year, an oracle declared that "Mikukurumitama" should be the deity's name, and the shrine was established to house this deity alongside four others.
=== Historical Records === The shrine may correspond to the "Kawachi Province Wani Shrine" mentioned in 850 CE, where the deity's rank was raised to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}}. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, it also served as a clan shrine for the {{ill|Kusunoki family|ja|楠木氏}}, who venerated the shrine as their ancestral deity.
The shrine was burned down during the {{ill|Siege of Akasaka|ja|赤坂城の戦い}} in 1331 but was later rebuilt under the support of {{ill|Kusunoki Masashige|ja|楠木正成}}. In 1585, it was again destroyed during the {{ill|Kishū Campaign|ja|紀州征伐}} by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}}. The current main shrine was reconstructed in 1660.
The shrine was designated as a gōsha in 1872 during the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}.
== Shrine Grounds == The shrine grounds include the following structures: * '''Main Shrine (Honden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Upper Worship Hall (Kami-haiden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Lower Worship Hall (Shimo-haiden):''' * '''Treasure Hall (Saigu-kura):''' * '''Gates and Torii:''' * '''Miyajinja Uramayama Kofun Group:''' Includes a keyhole-shaped kofun (58 m long) believed to be from the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Uramayama Kofun Explanation Board|publisher=Educational Board of Tondabayashi|accessdate=2020-08-28}}</ref>
== Subsidiary Shrines == * {{ill|Kumano Takahira Shrine|ja|熊野貴平神社}} * {{ill|Nagi Shrine|ja|南木神社}} – Dedicated to the Kusunoki family. * {{ill|Kuni no Ichi Shrine|ja|皇大神社}}
== Annual Festivals == The shrine's annual festival is held on the third Friday of October.

The shrine was burned down during the {{ill|Siege of Akasaka|ja|赤坂城の戦い}} in 1331 but was later rebuilt under the support of {{ill|Kusunoki Masashige|ja|楠木正成}}. In 1585, it was again destroyed during the {{ill|Kishū Campaign|ja|紀州征伐}} by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}}. The current main shrine was reconstructed in 1660.
The shrine was designated as a gōsha in 1872 during the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}.
== Shrine Grounds == The shrine grounds include the following structures: * '''Main Shrine (Honden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Upper Worship Hall (Kami-haiden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Lower Worship Hall (Shimo-haiden):''' * '''Treasure Hall (Saigu-kura):''' * '''Gates and Torii:''' * '''Miyajinja Uramayama Kofun Group:''' Includes a keyhole-shaped kofun (58 m long) believed to be from the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Uramayama Kofun Explanation Board|publisher=Educational Board of Tondabayashi|accessdate=2020-08-28}}</ref>
== Subsidiary Shrines == * {{ill|Kumano Takahira Shrine|ja|熊野貴平神社}} * {{ill|Nagi Shrine|ja|南木神社}} – Dedicated to the Kusunoki family. * {{ill|Kuni no Ichi Shrine|ja|皇大神社}}
== Annual Festivals == The shrine's annual festival is held on the third Friday of October.

'''Migukurumitama Shrine''' (美具久留御魂神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Tondabayashi|ja|富田林市}}, [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is an {{ill|historic shrine|ja|式内社}} and was formerly ranked as a {{ill|gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. The shrine is also selected as one of the {{ill|100 Green Selections of Osaka|ja|大阪みどりの百選}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/midorikikaku/toshiryokka/hyakusenn.html|title=Osaka 100 Green Selections|publisher=Osaka Prefecture|accessdate=2016-12-23}}</ref> It is also referred to as the "Shimono Suibun Shrine" (下水分社) and, along with {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine|ja|建水分神社}} (the Kamisuibun Shrine in {{ill|Chihayaakasaka|ja|千早赤阪村}}) and {{ill|Nishikori Shrine|ja|錦織神社}} (in Tondabayashi), it is one of the "Three Mikumari Shrines of {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}."
== Enshrined Deities == * '''Primary Deity:''' Migukurumitama no Ōkami (美具久留御魂大神) – The {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒魂・和魂}} (rough spirit) of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}}. It is believed to represent a water deity, deriving its name from "Mikukurumitama" (Water Swimming Spirit). * '''Additional Deities:''' * {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Amemikumari no Kami|ja|天水分神}} (Heavenly Mikumari Deity) * {{ill|Mizuhanome|lt=Mizuhanome no Mikoto|ja|弥都波迺売命}} * {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Kuni no Mikumari no Kami|ja|国水分神}} * {{ill|Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseribime no Mikoto|ja|須勢理比売命}} * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶比売神}}
== History == === Foundation === According to tradition, the shrine was founded in the 10th year of Emperor Sujin's reign (88 BCE). A giant serpent frequently appeared in the area, leading the emperor to identify it as the aramitama of Ōkuninushi. He dispatched {{ill|Izumo Furune|ja|出雲振根}} to enshrine the deity, borrowing sacred objects from {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|杵築大社}}.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Place Name Dictionary|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1986}}</ref>
In Emperor Sujin's 62nd year, an oracle declared that "Mikukurumitama" should be the deity's name, and the shrine was established to house this deity alongside four others.
=== Historical Records === The shrine may correspond to the "Kawachi Province Wani Shrine" mentioned in 850 CE, where the deity's rank was raised to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}}. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, it also served as a clan shrine for the {{ill|Kusunoki family|ja|楠木氏}}, who venerated the shrine as their ancestral deity.
The shrine was burned down during the {{ill|Siege of Akasaka|ja|赤坂城の戦い}} in 1331 but was later rebuilt under the support of {{ill|Kusunoki Masashige|ja|楠木正成}}. In 1585, it was again destroyed during the {{ill|Kishū Campaign|ja|紀州征伐}} by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}}. The current main shrine was reconstructed in 1660.
The shrine was designated as a gōsha in 1872 during the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}.
== Shrine Grounds == The shrine grounds include the following structures: * '''Main Shrine (Honden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Upper Worship Hall (Kami-haiden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Lower Worship Hall (Shimo-haiden):''' * '''Treasure Hall (Saigu-kura):''' * '''Gates and Torii:''' * '''Miyajinja Uramayama Kofun Group:''' Includes a keyhole-shaped kofun (58 m long) believed to be from the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Uramayama Kofun Explanation Board|publisher=Educational Board of Tondabayashi|accessdate=2020-08-28}}</ref>
== Subsidiary Shrines == * {{ill|Kumano Takahira Shrine|ja|熊野貴平神社}} * {{ill|Nagi Shrine|ja|南木神社}} – Dedicated to the Kusunoki family. * {{ill|Kuni no Ichi Shrine|ja|皇大神社}}
== Annual Festivals == The shrine's annual festival is held on the third Friday of October.

'''Migukurumitama Shrine''' (美具久留御魂神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Tondabayashi|ja|富田林市}}, [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is an {{ill|historic shrine|ja|式内社}} and was formerly ranked as a {{ill|gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. The shrine is also selected as one of the {{ill|100 Green Selections of Osaka|ja|大阪みどりの百選}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/midorikikaku/toshiryokka/hyakusenn.html|title=Osaka 100 Green Selections|publisher=Osaka Prefecture|accessdate=2016-12-23}}</ref> It is also referred to as the "Shimono Suibun Shrine" (下水分社) and, along with {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine}} (the Kamisuibun Shrine in {{ill|Chihayaakasaka|ja|千早赤阪村}}) and {{ill|Nishikori Shrine|ja|錦織神社}} (in Tondabayashi), it is one of the "Three Mikumari Shrines of {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}."
== Enshrined Deities == * '''Primary Deity:''' Migukurumitama no Ōkami (美具久留御魂大神) – The {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒魂・和魂}} (rough spirit) of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}}. It is believed to represent a water deity, deriving its name from "Mikukurumitama" (Water Swimming Spirit). * '''Additional Deities:''' * {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Amemikumari no Kami|ja|天水分神}} (Heavenly Mikumari Deity) * {{ill|Mizuhanome|lt=Mizuhanome no Mikoto|ja|弥都波迺売命}} * {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Kuni no Mikumari no Kami|ja|国水分神}} * {{ill|Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseribime no Mikoto|ja|須勢理比売命}} * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶比売神}}
== History == === Foundation === According to tradition, the shrine was founded in the 10th year of Emperor Sujin's reign (88 BCE). A giant serpent frequently appeared in the area, leading the emperor to identify it as the aramitama of Ōkuninushi. He dispatched {{ill|Izumo Furune|ja|出雲振根}} to enshrine the deity, borrowing sacred objects from {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|杵築大社}}.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Place Name Dictionary|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1986}}</ref>
In Emperor Sujin's 62nd year, an oracle declared that "Mikukurumitama" should be the deity's name, and the shrine was established to house this deity alongside four others.
=== Historical Records === The shrine may correspond to the "Kawachi Province Wani Shrine" mentioned in 850 CE, where the deity's rank was raised to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}}. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, it also served as a clan shrine for the {{ill|Kusunoki family|ja|楠木氏}}, who venerated the shrine as their ancestral deity.
The shrine was burned down during the {{ill|Siege of Akasaka|ja|赤坂城の戦い}} in 1331 but was later rebuilt under the support of {{ill|Kusunoki Masashige|ja|楠木正成}}. In 1585, it was again destroyed during the {{ill|Kishū Campaign|ja|紀州征伐}} by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}}. The current main shrine was reconstructed in 1660.
The shrine was designated as a gōsha in 1872 during the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}.
== Shrine Grounds == The shrine grounds include the following structures: * '''Main Shrine (Honden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Upper Worship Hall (Kami-haiden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Lower Worship Hall (Shimo-haiden):''' * '''Treasure Hall (Saigu-kura):''' * '''Gates and Torii:''' * '''Miyajinja Uramayama Kofun Group:''' Includes a keyhole-shaped kofun (58 m long) believed to be from the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Uramayama Kofun Explanation Board|publisher=Educational Board of Tondabayashi|accessdate=2020-08-28}}</ref>
== Subsidiary Shrines == * {{ill|Kumano Takahira Shrine|ja|熊野貴平神社}} * {{ill|Nagi Shrine|ja|南木神社}} – Dedicated to the Kusunoki family. * {{ill|Kuni no Ichi Shrine|ja|皇大神社}}
== Annual Festivals == The shrine's annual festival is held on the third Friday of October.

'''Migukurumitama Shrine''' (美具久留御魂神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Tondabayashi|ja|富田林市}}, [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is an {{ill|historic shrine|ja|式内社}} and was formerly ranked as a {{ill|gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. The shrine is also selected as one of the {{ill|100 Green Selections of Osaka|ja|大阪みどりの百選}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/midorikikaku/toshiryokka/hyakusenn.html|title=Osaka 100 Green Selections|publisher=Osaka Prefecture|accessdate=2016-12-23}}</ref> It is also referred to as the "Shimono Suibun Shrine" (下水分社) and, along with {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine}} (the Kamisuibun Shrine in {{ill|Chihayaakasaka|ja|千早赤阪村}}) and {{ill|Nishikori Shrine|ja|錦織神社}} (in Tondabayashi), it is one of the "Three Mikumari Shrines of {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}."
== Enshrined Deities == * '''Primary Deity:''' Migukurumitama no Ōkami (美具久留御魂大神) – The {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒魂・和魂}} (rough spirit) of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}}. It is believed to represent a water deity, deriving its name from "Mikukurumitama" (Water Swimming Spirit). * '''Additional Deities:''' * {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Amemikumari no Kami|ja|天水分神}} (Heavenly Mikumari Deity) * {{ill|Mizuhanome|lt=Mizuhanome no Mikoto|ja|弥都波迺売命}} * {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Kuni no Mikumari no Kami|ja|国水分神}} * {{ill|Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseribime no Mikoto|ja|須勢理比売命}} * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶比売神}}
== History == === Foundation === According to tradition, the shrine was founded in the 10th year of Emperor Sujin's reign (88 BCE). A giant serpent frequently appeared in the area, leading the emperor to identify it as the aramitama of Ōkuninushi. He dispatched {{ill|Izumo Furune|ja|出雲振根}} to enshrine the deity, borrowing sacred objects from {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|杵築大社}}.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Place Name Dictionary|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1986}}</ref>
In Emperor Sujin's 62nd year, an oracle declared that "Mikukurumitama" should be the deity's name, and the shrine was established to house this deity alongside four others.
=== Historical Records === The shrine may correspond to the "Kawachi Province Wani Shrine" mentioned in 850 CE, where the deity's rank was raised to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}}. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, it also served as a clan shrine for the {{ill|Kusunoki family|ja|楠木氏}}, who venerated the shrine as their ancestral deity.
The shrine was burned down during the {{ill|Siege of Akasaka|ja|赤坂城の戦い}} in 1331 but was later rebuilt under the support of {{ill|Kusunoki Masashige|ja|楠木正成}}. In 1585, it was again destroyed during the {{ill|Kishū Campaign|ja|紀州征伐}} by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}}. The current main shrine was reconstructed in 1660.
The shrine was designated as a gōsha in 1872 during the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}.
== Shrine Grounds == The shrine grounds include the following structures: * '''Main Shrine (Honden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Upper Worship Hall (Kami-haiden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Lower Worship Hall (Shimo-haiden):''' * '''Treasure Hall (Saigu-kura):''' * '''Gates and Torii:''' * '''Miyajinja Uramayama Kofun Group:''' Includes a keyhole-shaped kofun (58 m long) believed to be from the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Uramayama Kofun Explanation Board|publisher=Educational Board of Tondabayashi|accessdate=2020-08-28}}</ref>
== Subsidiary Shrines == * {{ill|Kumano Takahira Shrine|ja|熊野貴平神社}} * {{ill|Nagi Shrine|ja|南木神社}} – Dedicated to the Kusunoki family. * {{ill|Kuni no Ichi Shrine|ja|皇大神社}}
== Annual Festivals == The shrine's annual festival is held on the third Friday of October.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine}}
== External Links == * [https://osaka-info.jp/page/migukurumitamajinja Migukurumitama Shrine | OSAKA-INFO]

The shrine is also selected as one of the {{ill|100 Green Selections of Osaka|ja|大阪みどりの百選}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/midorikikaku/toshiryokka/hyakusenn.html|title=Osaka 100 Green Selections|publisher=Osaka Prefecture|accessdate=2016-12-23}}</ref> It is also referred to as the "Shimono Suibun Shrine" (下水分社) and, along with {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine}} (the Kamisuibun Shrine in {{ill|Chihayaakasaka|ja|千早赤阪村}}) and {{ill|Nishikori Shrine|ja|錦織神社}} (in Tondabayashi), it is one of the "Three Mikumari Shrines of {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}."

It is believed to represent a water deity, deriving its name from "Mikukurumitama" (Water Swimming Spirit). * '''Additional Deities:''' * {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Amemikumari no Kami|ja|天水分神}} (Heavenly Mikumari Deity) * {{ill|Mizuhanome|lt=Mizuhanome no Mikoto|ja|弥都波迺売命}} * {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Kuni no Mikumari no Kami|ja|国水分神}} * {{ill|Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseribime no Mikoto|ja|須勢理比売命}} * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶比売神}}
== History == === Foundation === According to tradition, the shrine was founded in the 10th year of Emperor Sujin's reign (88 BCE). A giant serpent frequently appeared in the area, leading the emperor to identify it as the aramitama of Ōkuninushi. He dispatched {{ill|Izumo Furune|ja|出雲振根}} to enshrine the deity, borrowing sacred objects from {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|杵築大社}}.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Place Name Dictionary|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1986}}</ref>
In Emperor Sujin's 62nd year, an oracle declared that "Mikukurumitama" should be the deity's name, and the shrine was established to house this deity alongside four others.
=== Historical Records === The shrine may correspond to the "Kawachi Province Wani Shrine" mentioned in 850 CE, where the deity's rank was raised to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, it also served as a clan shrine for the {{ill|Kusunoki family|ja|楠木氏}}, who venerated the shrine as their ancestral deity.
The shrine was burned down during the {{ill|Siege of Akasaka|ja|赤坂城の戦い}} in 1331 but was later rebuilt under the support of {{ill|Kusunoki Masashige|ja|楠木正成}}. In 1585, it was again destroyed during the {{ill|Kishū Campaign|ja|紀州征伐}} by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}}. The current main shrine was reconstructed in 1660.
The shrine was designated as a gōsha in 1872 during the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}.
== Shrine Grounds == The shrine grounds include the following structures: * '''Main Shrine (Honden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Upper Worship Hall (Kami-haiden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Lower Worship Hall (Shimo-haiden):''' * '''Treasure Hall (Saigu-kura):''' * '''Gates and Torii:''' * '''Miyajinja Uramayama Kofun Group:''' Includes a keyhole-shaped kofun (58 m long) believed to be from the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Uramayama Kofun Explanation Board|publisher=Educational Board of Tondabayashi|accessdate=2020-08-28}}</ref>
== Subsidiary Shrines == * {{ill|Kumano Takahira Shrine|ja|熊野貴平神社}} * {{ill|Nagi Shrine|ja|南木神社}} – Dedicated to the Kusunoki family. * {{ill|Kuni no Ichi Shrine|ja|皇大神社}}
== Annual Festivals == The shrine's annual festival is held on the third Friday of October.

'''Migukurumitama Shrine''' (美具久留御魂神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Tondabayashi|ja|富田林市}}, [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is an {{ill|historic shrine|ja|式内社}} and was formerly ranked as a {{ill|gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. The shrine is also selected as one of the {{ill|100 Green Selections of Osaka|ja|大阪みどりの百選}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/midorikikaku/toshiryokka/hyakusenn.html|title=Osaka 100 Green Selections|publisher=Osaka Prefecture|accessdate=2016-12-23}}</ref> It is also referred to as the "Shimono Suibun Shrine" (下水分社) and, along with {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine}} (the Kamisuibun Shrine in {{ill|Chihayaakasaka|ja|千早赤阪村}}) and {{ill|Nishikori Shrine|ja|錦織神社}} (in Tondabayashi), it is one of the "Three Mikumari Shrines of {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}."
== Enshrined Deities == * '''Primary Deity:''' Migukurumitama no Ōkami (美具久留御魂大神) – The {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒魂・和魂}} (rough spirit) of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}}. It is believed to represent a water deity, deriving its name from "Mikukurumitama" (Water Swimming Spirit). * '''Additional Deities:''' * {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Amemikumari no Kami|ja|天水分神}} (Heavenly Mikumari Deity) * {{ill|Mizuhanome|lt=Mizuhanome no Mikoto|ja|弥都波迺売命}} * {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Kuni no Mikumari no Kami|ja|国水分神}} * {{ill|Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseribime no Mikoto|ja|須勢理比売命}} * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶比売神}}
== History == === Foundation === According to tradition, the shrine was founded in the 10th year of Emperor Sujin's reign (88 BCE). A giant serpent frequently appeared in the area, leading the emperor to identify it as the aramitama of Ōkuninushi. He dispatched {{ill|Izumo Furune|ja|出雲振根}} to enshrine the deity, borrowing sacred objects from {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|杵築大社}}.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Place Name Dictionary|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1986}}</ref>
In Emperor Sujin's 62nd year, an oracle declared that "Mikukurumitama" should be the deity's name, and the shrine was established to house this deity alongside four others.
=== Historical Records === The shrine may correspond to the "Kawachi Province Wani Shrine" mentioned in 850 CE, where the deity's rank was raised to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, it also served as a clan shrine for the {{ill|Kusunoki family|ja|楠木氏}}, who venerated the shrine as their ancestral deity.
The shrine was burned down during the {{ill|Siege of Akasaka|ja|赤坂城の戦い}} in 1331 but was later rebuilt under the support of {{ill|Kusunoki Masashige|ja|楠木正成}}. In 1585, it was again destroyed during the {{ill|Kishū Campaign|ja|紀州征伐}} by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}}. The current main shrine was reconstructed in 1660.
The shrine was designated as a gōsha in 1872 during the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}.
== Shrine Grounds == The shrine grounds include the following structures: * '''Main Shrine (Honden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Upper Worship Hall (Kami-haiden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Lower Worship Hall (Shimo-haiden):''' * '''Treasure Hall (Saigu-kura):''' * '''Gates and Torii:''' * '''Miyajinja Uramayama Kofun Group:''' Includes a keyhole-shaped kofun (58 m long) believed to be from the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Uramayama Kofun Explanation Board|publisher=Educational Board of Tondabayashi|accessdate=2020-08-28}}</ref>
== Subsidiary Shrines == * {{ill|Kumano Takahira Shrine|ja|熊野貴平神社}} * {{ill|Nagi Shrine|ja|南木神社}} – Dedicated to the Kusunoki family. * {{ill|Kuni no Ichi Shrine|ja|皇大神社}}
== Annual Festivals == The shrine's annual festival is held on the third Friday of October.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine}}
== External Links == * [https://osaka-info.jp/page/migukurumitamajinja Migukurumitama Shrine | OSAKA-INFO]

The shrine is also selected as one of the {{ill|100 Green Selections of Osaka|ja|大阪みどりの百選}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/midorikikaku/toshiryokka/hyakusenn.html|title=Osaka 100 Green Selections|publisher=Osaka Prefecture|accessdate=2016-12-23}}</ref> It is also referred to as the "Shimono Suibun Shrine" (下水分社) and, along with {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine}} (the Kamisuibun Shrine in {{ill|Chihayaakasaka|ja|千早赤阪村}}) and {{ill|Nishikori Shrine|ja|錦織神社}} (in Tondabayashi), it is one of the "Three Mikumari Shrines of {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}."

It is believed to represent a water deity, deriving its name from "Mikukurumitama" (Water Swimming Spirit). * '''Additional Deities:''' * {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Amemikumari no Kami|ja|天水分神}} (Heavenly Mikumari Deity) * {{ill|Mizuhanome|lt=Mizuhanome no Mikoto|ja|弥都波迺売命}} * {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Kuni no Mikumari no Kami|ja|国水分神}} * {{ill|Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseribime no Mikoto|ja|須勢理比売命}} * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶比売神}}
== History == === Foundation === According to tradition, the shrine was founded in the 10th year of Emperor Sujin's reign (88 BCE). A giant serpent frequently appeared in the area, leading the emperor to identify it as the aramitama of Ōkuninushi. He dispatched {{ill|Izumo Furune|ja|出雲振根}} to enshrine the deity, borrowing sacred objects from {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|杵築大社}}.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Place Name Dictionary|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1986}}</ref>
In Emperor Sujin's 62nd year, an oracle declared that "Mikukurumitama" should be the deity's name, and the shrine was established to house this deity alongside four others.
=== Historical Records === The shrine may correspond to the "Kawachi Province Wani Shrine" mentioned in 850 CE, where the deity's rank was raised to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, it also served as a clan shrine for the {{ill|Kusunoki family|ja|楠木氏}}, who venerated the shrine as their ancestral deity.
The shrine was burned down during the {{ill|Siege of Akasaka|ja|赤坂城の戦い}} in 1331 but was later rebuilt under the support of {{ill|Kusunoki Masashige|ja|楠木正成}}. In 1585, it was again destroyed during the {{ill|Kishū Campaign|ja|紀州征伐}} by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}}. The current main shrine was reconstructed in 1660.
The shrine was designated as a gōsha in 1872 during the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}.
== Shrine Grounds == The shrine grounds include the following structures: * '''Main Shrine (Honden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Upper Worship Hall (Kami-haiden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Lower Worship Hall (Shimo-haiden):''' * '''Treasure Hall (Saigu-kura):''' * '''Gates and Torii:''' * '''Miyajinja Uramayama Kofun Group:''' Includes a keyhole-shaped kofun (58 m long) believed to be from the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Uramayama Kofun Explanation Board|publisher=Educational Board of Tondabayashi|accessdate=2020-08-28}}</ref>
== Subsidiary Shrines == * {{ill|Kumano Takahira Shrine|ja|熊野貴平神社}} * {{ill|Nagi Shrine|ja|南木神社}} – Dedicated to the Kusunoki family. * {{ill|Kuni no Ichi Shrine|ja|皇大神社}}
== Annual Festivals == The shrine's annual festival is held on the third Friday of October.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine}}
== External Links == * [https://osaka-info.jp/page/migukurumitamajinja Migukurumitama Shrine | OSAKA-INFO]

revision.text:
'''Migukurumitama Shrine''' (美具久留御魂神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Tondabayashi|ja|富田林市}}, [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is an {{ill|historic shrine|ja|式内社}} and was formerly ranked as a {{ill|gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. The shrine is also selected as one of the {{ill|100 Green Selections of Osaka|ja|大阪みどりの百選}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/midorikikaku/toshiryokka/hyakusenn.html|title=Osaka 100 Green Selections|publisher=Osaka Prefecture|accessdate=2016-12-23}}</ref> It is also referred to as the "Shimono Suibun Shrine" (下水分社) and, along with {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine}} (the Kamisuibun Shrine in {{ill|Chihayaakasaka|ja|千早赤阪村}}) and {{ill|Nishikori Shrine|ja|錦織神社}} (in Tondabayashi), it is one of the "Three Mikumari Shrines of {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}."

It is believed to represent a water deity, deriving its name from "Mikukurumitama" (Water Swimming Spirit). * '''Additional Deities:''' * {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Amemikumari no Kami|ja|天水分神}} (Heavenly Mikumari Deity) * {{ill|Mizuhanome|lt=Mizuhanome no Mikoto|ja|弥都波迺売命}} * {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Kuni no Mikumari no Kami|ja|国水分神}} * {{ill|Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseribime no Mikoto|ja|須勢理比売命}} * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶比売神}}

A giant serpent frequently appeared in the area, leading the emperor to identify it as the aramitama of Ōkuninushi. The shrine was burned down during the {{ill|Siege of Akasaka|ja|赤坂城の戦い}} in 1331 but was later rebuilt under the support of {{ill|Kusunoki Masashige|ja|楠木正成}}. In 1585, it was again destroyed during the {{ill|Kishū Campaign|ja|紀州征伐}} by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}}. The current main shrine was reconstructed in 1660.
The shrine was designated as a gōsha in 1872 during the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}.

== Shrine Grounds == The shrine grounds include the following structures: * '''Main Shrine (Honden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Upper Worship Hall (Kami-haiden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Lower Worship Hall (Shimo-haiden):''' * '''Treasure Hall (Saigu-kura):''' * '''Gates and Torii:''' * '''Miyajinja Uramayama Kofun Group:''' Includes a keyhole-shaped kofun (58 m long) believed to be from the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Uramayama Kofun Explanation Board|publisher=Educational Board of Tondabayashi|accessdate=2020-08-28}}</ref>
== Subsidiary Shrines == * {{ill|Kumano Takahira Shrine|ja|熊野貴平神社}} * {{ill|Nagi Shrine|ja|南木神社}} – Dedicated to the Kusunoki family. * {{ill|Kuni no Ichi Shrine|ja|皇大神社}}
== Annual Festivals == The shrine's annual festival is held on the third Friday of October.

'''Migukurumitama Shrine''' (美具久留御魂神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Tondabayashi|ja|富田林市}}, [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is an {{ill|historic shrine|ja|式内社}} and was formerly ranked as a {{ill|gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. The shrine is also selected as one of the {{ill|100 Green Selections of Osaka|ja|大阪みどりの百選}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/midorikikaku/toshiryokka/hyakusenn.html|title=Osaka 100 Green Selections|publisher=Osaka Prefecture|accessdate=2016-12-23}}</ref> It is also referred to as the "Shimono Suibun Shrine" (下水分社) and, along with {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine}} (the Kamisuibun Shrine in {{ill|Chihayaakasaka|ja|千早赤阪村}}) and {{ill|Nishikori Shrine|ja|錦織神社}} (in Tondabayashi), it is one of the "Three Mikumari Shrines of {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}."
== Enshrined Deities == * '''Primary Deity:''' Migukurumitama no Ōkami (美具久留御魂大神) – The {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒魂・和魂}} (rough spirit) of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}}. It is believed to represent a water deity, deriving its name from "Mikukurumitama" (Water Swimming Spirit). * '''Additional Deities:''' * {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Amemikumari no Kami|ja|天水分神}} (Heavenly Mikumari Deity) * {{ill|Mizuhanome|lt=Mizuhanome no Mikoto|ja|弥都波迺売命}} * {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Kuni no Mikumari no Kami|ja|国水分神}} * {{ill|Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseribime no Mikoto|ja|須勢理比売命}} * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶比売神}}
== History == === Foundation === According to tradition, the shrine was founded in the 10th year of Emperor Sujin's reign (88 BCE). A giant serpent frequently appeared in the area, leading the emperor to identify it as the aramitama of Ōkuninushi. He dispatched {{ill|Izumo Furune|ja|出雲振根}} to enshrine the deity, borrowing sacred objects from {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|杵築大社}}.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Place Name Dictionary|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1986}}</ref>
In Emperor Sujin's 62nd year, an oracle declared that "Mikukurumitama" should be the deity's name, and the shrine was established to house this deity alongside four others.
=== Historical Records === The shrine may correspond to the "Kawachi Province Wani Shrine" mentioned in 850 CE, where the deity's rank was raised to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, it also served as a clan shrine for the {{ill|Kusunoki family|ja|楠木氏}}, who venerated the shrine as their ancestral deity.
The shrine was burned down during the {{ill|Siege of Akasaka|ja|赤坂城の戦い}} in 1331 but was later rebuilt under the support of {{ill|Kusunoki Masashige|ja|楠木正成}}. In 1585, it was again destroyed during the {{ill|Kishū Campaign|ja|紀州征伐}} by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}}. The current main shrine was reconstructed in 1660.
The shrine was designated as a gōsha in 1872 during the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}.
== Shrine Grounds == The shrine grounds include the following structures: * '''Main Shrine (Honden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Upper Worship Hall (Kami-haiden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Lower Worship Hall (Shimo-haiden):''' * '''Treasure Hall (Saigu-kura):''' * '''Gates and Torii:''' * '''Miyajinja Uramayama Kofun Group:''' Includes a keyhole-shaped kofun (58 m long) believed to be from the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Uramayama Kofun Explanation Board|publisher=Educational Board of Tondabayashi|accessdate=2020-08-28}}</ref>
== Subsidiary Shrines == * {{ill|Kumano Takahira Shrine|ja|熊野貴平神社}} * {{ill|Nagi Shrine|ja|南木神社}} – Dedicated to the Kusunoki family. * {{ill|Kuni no Ichi Shrine|ja|皇大神社}}
== Annual Festivals == The shrine's annual festival is held on the third Friday of October.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine}}
== External Links == * [https://osaka-info.jp/page/migukurumitamajinja Migukurumitama Shrine | OSAKA-INFO]

'''Migukurumitama Shrine''' (美具久留御魂神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Tondabayashi|ja|富田林市}}, [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is an {{ill|historic shrine|ja|式内社}} and was formerly ranked as a {{ill|gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. The shrine is also selected as one of the {{ill|100 Green Selections of Osaka|ja|大阪みどりの百選}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/midorikikaku/toshiryokka/hyakusenn.html|title=Osaka 100 Green Selections|publisher=Osaka Prefecture|accessdate=2016-12-23}}</ref> It is also referred to as the "Shimono Suibun Shrine" (下水分社) and, along with {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine}} (the Kamisuibun Shrine in {{ill|Chihayaakasaka|ja|千早赤阪村}}) and {{ill|Nishikori Shrine|ja|錦織神社}} (in Tondabayashi), it is one of the "Three Mikumari Shrines of {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}."
== Enshrined Deities == * '''Primary Deity:''' Migukurumitama no Ōkami (美具久留御魂大神) – The {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒魂・和魂}} (rough spirit) of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}}. It is believed to represent a water deity, deriving its name from "Mikukurumitama" (Water Swimming Spirit). * '''Additional Deities:''' * {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Amemikumari no Kami|ja|天水分神}} (Heavenly Mikumari Deity) * {{ill|Mizuhanome|lt=Mizuhanome no Mikoto|ja|弥都波迺売命}} * {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Kuni no Mikumari no Kami|ja|国水分神}} * {{ill|Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseribime no Mikoto|ja|須勢理比売命}} * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶比売神}}
== History == === Foundation === According to tradition, the shrine was founded in the 10th year of Emperor Sujin's reign (88 BCE). A giant serpent frequently appeared in the area, leading the emperor to identify it as the aramitama of Ōkuninushi. He dispatched {{ill|Izumo Furune|ja|出雲振根}} to enshrine the deity, borrowing sacred objects from {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|杵築大社}}.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Place Name Dictionary|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1986}}</ref>
In Emperor Sujin's 62nd year, an oracle declared that "Mikukurumitama" should be the deity's name, and the shrine was established to house this deity alongside four others.
=== Historical Records === The shrine may correspond to the "Kawachi Province Wani Shrine" mentioned in 850 CE, where the deity's rank was raised to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, it also served as a clan shrine for the {{ill|Kusunoki family|ja|楠木氏}}, who venerated the shrine as their ancestral deity.
The shrine was burned down during the {{ill|Siege of Akasaka|ja|赤坂城の戦い}} in 1331 but was later rebuilt under the support of {{ill|Kusunoki Masashige|ja|楠木正成}}. In 1585, it was again destroyed during the {{ill|Kishū Campaign|ja|紀州征伐}} by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}}. The current main shrine was reconstructed in 1660.
The shrine was designated as a gōsha in 1872 during the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}.
== Shrine Grounds == The shrine grounds include the following structures: * '''Main Shrine (Honden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Upper Worship Hall (Kami-haiden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Lower Worship Hall (Shimo-haiden):''' * '''Treasure Hall (Saigu-kura):''' * '''Gates and Torii:''' * '''Miyajinja Uramayama Kofun Group:''' Includes a keyhole-shaped kofun (58 m long) believed to be from the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Uramayama Kofun Explanation Board|publisher=Educational Board of Tondabayashi|accessdate=2020-08-28}}</ref>
== Subsidiary Shrines == * {{ill|Kumano Takahira Shrine|ja|熊野貴平神社}} * {{ill|Nagi Shrine|ja|南木神社}} – Dedicated to the Kusunoki family. * {{ill|Kuni no Ichi Shrine|ja|皇大神社}}
== Annual Festivals == The shrine's annual festival is held on the third Friday of October.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine}}
== External Links == * [https://osaka-info.jp/page/migukurumitamajinja Migukurumitama Shrine | OSAKA-INFO]

It is an {{ill|historic shrine|ja|式内社}} and was formerly ranked as a {{ill|gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. The shrine is also selected as one of the {{ill|100 Green Selections of Osaka|ja|大阪みどりの百選}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/midorikikaku/toshiryokka/hyakusenn.html|title=Osaka 100 Green Selections|publisher=Osaka Prefecture|accessdate=2016-12-23}}</ref> It is also referred to as the "Shimono Suibun Shrine" (下水分社) and, along with {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine}} (the Kamisuibun Shrine in {{ill|Chihayaakasaka|ja|千早赤阪村}}) and {{ill|Nishikori Shrine|ja|錦織神社}} (in Tondabayashi), it is one of the "Three Mikumari Shrines of {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}."
== Enshrined Deities == * '''Primary Deity:''' Migukurumitama no Ōkami (美具久留御魂大神) – The {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒魂・和魂}} (rough spirit) of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}}. It is believed to represent a water deity, deriving its name from "Mikukurumitama" (Water Swimming Spirit). * '''Additional Deities:''' * {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Amemikumari no Kami|ja|天水分神}} (Heavenly Mikumari Deity) * {{ill|Mizuhanome|lt=Mizuhanome no Mikoto|ja|弥都波迺売命}} * {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Kuni no Mikumari no Kami|ja|国水分神}} * {{ill|Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseribime no Mikoto|ja|須勢理比売命}} * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶比売神}}
== History == === Foundation === According to tradition, the shrine was founded in the 10th year of Emperor Sujin's reign (88 BCE). A giant serpent frequently appeared in the area, leading the emperor to identify it as the aramitama of Ōkuninushi. He dispatched {{ill|Izumo Furune|ja|出雲振根}} to enshrine the deity, borrowing sacred objects from {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|杵築大社}}.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Place Name Dictionary|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1986}}</ref>
In Emperor Sujin's 62nd year, an oracle declared that "Mikukurumitama" should be the deity's name, and the shrine was established to house this deity alongside four others.
=== Historical Records === The shrine may correspond to the "Kawachi Province Wani Shrine" mentioned in 850 CE, where the deity's rank was raised to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, it also served as a clan shrine for the {{ill|Kusunoki family|ja|楠木氏}}, who venerated the shrine as their ancestral deity.
The shrine was burned down during the {{ill|Siege of Akasaka|ja|赤坂城の戦い}} in 1331 but was later rebuilt under the support of {{ill|Kusunoki Masashige|ja|楠木正成}}. In 1585, it was again destroyed during the {{ill|Kishū Campaign|ja|紀州征伐}} by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}}. The current main shrine was reconstructed in 1660.
The shrine was designated as a gōsha in 1872 during the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}.
== Shrine Grounds == The shrine grounds include the following structures: * '''Main Shrine (Honden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Upper Worship Hall (Kami-haiden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Lower Worship Hall (Shimo-haiden):''' * '''Treasure Hall (Saigu-kura):''' * '''Gates and Torii:''' * '''Miyajinja Uramayama Kofun Group:''' Includes a keyhole-shaped kofun (58 m long) believed to be from the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Uramayama Kofun Explanation Board|publisher=Educational Board of Tondabayashi|accessdate=2020-08-28}}</ref>
== Subsidiary Shrines == * {{ill|Kumano Takahira Shrine|ja|熊野貴平神社}} * {{ill|Nagi Shrine|ja|南木神社}} – Dedicated to the Kusunoki family. * {{ill|Kuni no Ichi Shrine|ja|皇大神社}}
== Annual Festivals == The shrine's annual festival is held on the third Friday of October.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine}}
== External Links == * [https://osaka-info.jp/page/migukurumitamajinja Migukurumitama Shrine | OSAKA-INFO]

revision.text:
'''Migukurumitama Shrine''' (美具久留御魂神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Tondabayashi|ja|富田林市}}, [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is an {{ill|historic shrine|ja|式内社}} and was formerly ranked as a {{ill|gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. The shrine is also selected as one of the {{ill|100 Green Selections of Osaka|ja|大阪みどりの百選}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/midorikikaku/toshiryokka/hyakusenn.html|title=Osaka 100 Green Selections|publisher=Osaka Prefecture|accessdate=2016-12-23}}</ref> It is also referred to as the "Shimono Suibun Shrine" (下水分社) and, along with {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine}} (the Kamisuibun Shrine in {{ill|Chihayaakasaka|ja|千早赤阪村}}) and {{ill|Nishikori Shrine|ja|錦織神社}} (in Tondabayashi), it is one of the "Three Mikumari Shrines of {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}."
== Enshrined Deities == * '''Primary Deity:''' Migukurumitama no Ōkami (美具久留御魂大神) – The {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒魂・和魂}} (rough spirit) of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}}. It is believed to represent a water deity, deriving its name from "Mikukurumitama" (Water Swimming Spirit). * '''Additional Deities:''' ** {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Amemikumari no Kami|ja|天水分神}} (Heavenly Mikumari Deity) ** {{ill|Mizuhanome|lt=Mizuhanome no Mikoto|ja|弥都波迺売命}} ** {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Kuni no Mikumari no Kami|ja|国水分神}} ** {{ill|Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseribime no Mikoto|ja|須勢理比売命}} ** {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶比売神}}
== History == === Foundation === According to tradition, the shrine was founded in the 10th year of Emperor Sujin's reign (88 BCE). A giant serpent frequently appeared in the area, leading the emperor to identify it as the aramitama of Ōkuninushi. He dispatched {{ill|Izumo Furune|ja|出雲振根}} to enshrine the deity, borrowing sacred objects from {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|杵築大社}}.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Place Name Dictionary|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1986}}</ref>
In Emperor Sujin's 62nd year, an oracle declared that "Mikukurumitama" should be the deity's name, and the shrine was established to house this deity alongside four others.
=== Historical Records === The shrine may correspond to the "Kawachi Province Wani Shrine" mentioned in 850 CE, where the deity's rank was raised to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, it also served as a clan shrine for the {{ill|Kusunoki family|ja|楠木氏}}, who venerated the shrine as their ancestral deity.
The shrine was burned down during the {{ill|Siege of Akasaka|ja|赤坂城の戦い}} in 1331 but was later rebuilt under the support of {{ill|Kusunoki Masashige|ja|楠木正成}}. In 1585, it was again destroyed during the {{ill|Kishū Campaign|ja|紀州征伐}} by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}. The current main shrine was reconstructed in 1660.
The shrine was designated as a gōsha in 1872 during the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}.
== Shrine Grounds == The shrine grounds include the following structures: * '''Main Shrine (Honden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Upper Worship Hall (Kami-haiden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Lower Worship Hall (Shimo-haiden):''' * '''Treasure Hall (Saigu-kura):''' * '''Gates and Torii:''' * '''Miyajinja Uramayama Kofun Group:''' Includes a keyhole-shaped kofun (58 m long) believed to be from the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Uramayama Kofun Explanation Board|publisher=Educational Board of Tondabayashi|accessdate=2020-08-28}}</ref>
== Subsidiary Shrines == * {{ill|Kumano Takahira Shrine|ja|熊野貴平神社}} * {{ill|Nagi Shrine|ja|南木神社}} – Dedicated to the Kusunoki family. * {{ill|Kuni no Ichi Shrine|ja|皇大神社}}
== Annual Festivals == The shrine's annual festival is held on the third Friday of October.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine}}
== External Links == * [https://osaka-info.jp/page/migukurumitamajinja Migukurumitama Shrine | OSAKA-INFO]

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Completion tier 2]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Migukurumitama Shrine''' (美具久留御魂神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Tondabayashi|ja|富田林市}}, [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is an {{ill|historic shrine|ja|式内社}} and was formerly ranked as a {{ill|gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. The shrine is also selected as one of the {{ill|100 Green Selections of Osaka|ja|大阪みどりの百選}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/midorikikaku/toshiryokka/hyakusenn.html|title=Osaka 100 Green Selections|publisher=Osaka Prefecture|accessdate=2016-12-23}}</ref> It is also referred to as the "Shimono Suibun Shrine" (下水分社) and, along with {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine}} (the Kamisuibun Shrine in {{ill|Chihayaakasaka|ja|千早赤阪村}}) and {{ill|Nishikori Shrine|ja|錦織神社}} (in Tondabayashi), it is one of the "Three Mikumari Shrines of {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}."
== Enshrined Deities == * '''Primary Deity:''' Migukurumitama no Ōkami (美具久留御魂大神) – The {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒魂・和魂}} (rough spirit) of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}}. It is believed to represent a water deity, deriving its name from "Mikukurumitama" (Water Swimming Spirit). * '''Additional Deities:''' ** {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Amemikumari no Kami|ja|天水分神}} (Heavenly Mikumari Deity) ** {{ill|Mizuhanome|lt=Mizuhanome no Mikoto|ja|弥都波迺売命}} ** {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Kuni no Mikumari no Kami|ja|国水分神}} ** {{ill|Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseribime no Mikoto|ja|須勢理比売命}} ** {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶比売神}}
== History == === Foundation === According to tradition, the shrine was founded in the 10th year of Emperor Sujin's reign (88 BCE). A giant serpent frequently appeared in the area, leading the emperor to identify it as the aramitama of Ōkuninushi. He dispatched {{ill|Izumo Furune|ja|出雲振根}} to enshrine the deity, borrowing sacred objects from {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|杵築大社}}.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Place Name Dictionary|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1986}}</ref>
In Emperor Sujin's 62nd year, an oracle declared that "Mikukurumitama" should be the deity's name, and the shrine was established to house this deity alongside four others.
=== Historical Records === The shrine may correspond to the "Kawachi Province Wani Shrine" mentioned in 850 CE, where the deity's rank was raised to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, it also served as a clan shrine for the {{ill|Kusunoki family|ja|楠木氏}}, who venerated the shrine as their ancestral deity.
The shrine was burned down during the {{ill|Siege of Akasaka|ja|赤坂城の戦い}} in 1331 but was later rebuilt under the support of {{ill|Kusunoki Masashige|ja|楠木正成}}. In 1585, it was again destroyed during the {{ill|Kishū Campaign|ja|紀州征伐}} by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}. The current main shrine was reconstructed in 1660.
The shrine was designated as a gōsha in 1872 during the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}.
== Shrine Grounds == The shrine grounds include the following structures: * '''Main Shrine (Honden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Upper Worship Hall (Kami-haiden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Lower Worship Hall (Shimo-haiden):''' * '''Treasure Hall (Saigu-kura):''' * '''Gates and Torii:''' * '''Miyajinja Uramayama Kofun Group:''' Includes a keyhole-shaped kofun (58 m long) believed to be from the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Uramayama Kofun Explanation Board|publisher=Educational Board of Tondabayashi|accessdate=2020-08-28}}</ref>
== Subsidiary Shrines == * {{ill|Kumano Takahira Shrine|ja|熊野貴平神社}} * {{ill|Nagi Shrine|ja|南木神社}} – Dedicated to the Kusunoki family. * {{ill|Kuni no Ichi Shrine|ja|皇大神社}}
== Annual Festivals == The shrine's annual festival is held on the third Friday of October.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine}}
== External Links == * [https://osaka-info.jp/page/migukurumitamajinja Migukurumitama Shrine | OSAKA-INFO]

It is believed to represent a water deity, deriving its name from "Mikukurumitama" (Water Swimming Spirit). * '''Additional Deities:''' ** {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Amemikumari no Kami|ja|天水分神}} (Heavenly Mikumari Deity) ** {{ill|Mizuhanome|lt=Mizuhanome no Mikoto|ja|弥都波迺売命}} ** {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Kuni no Mikumari no Kami|ja|国水分神}} ** {{ill|Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseribime no Mikoto|ja|須勢理比売命}} ** {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶比売神}}

'''Migukurumitama Shrine''' (美具久留御魂神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Tondabayashi|ja|富田林市}}, [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is an {{ill|historic shrine|ja|式内社}} and was formerly ranked as a {{ill|gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. The shrine is also selected as one of the {{ill|100 Green Selections of Osaka|ja|大阪みどりの百選}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/midorikikaku/toshiryokka/hyakusenn.html|title=Osaka 100 Green Selections|publisher=Osaka Prefecture|accessdate=2016-12-23}}</ref> It is also referred to as the "Shimono Suibun Shrine" (下水分社) and, along with {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine}} (the Kamisuibun Shrine in {{ill|Chihayaakasaka|ja|千早赤阪村}}) and {{ill|Nishikori Shrine|ja|錦織神社}} (in Tondabayashi), it is one of the "Three Mikumari Shrines of {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}."
== Enshrined Deities == * '''Primary Deity:''' Migukurumitama no Ōkami (美具久留御魂大神) – The {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒魂・和魂}} (rough spirit) of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}}. It is believed to represent a water deity, deriving its name from "Mikukurumitama" (Water Swimming Spirit). * '''Additional Deities:''' ** {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Amemikumari no Kami|ja|天水分神}} (Heavenly Mikumari Deity) ** {{ill|Mizuhanome|lt=Mizuhanome no Mikoto|ja|弥都波迺売命}} ** {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Kuni no Mikumari no Kami|ja|国水分神}} ** {{ill|Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseribime no Mikoto|ja|須勢理比売命}} ** {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶比売神}}
== History == === Foundation === According to tradition, the shrine was founded in the 10th year of Emperor Sujin's reign (88 BCE). A giant serpent frequently appeared in the area, leading the emperor to identify it as the aramitama of Ōkuninushi. He dispatched {{ill|Izumo Furune|ja|出雲振根}} to enshrine the deity, borrowing sacred objects from {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|杵築大社}}.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Place Name Dictionary|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1986}}</ref>
In Emperor Sujin's 62nd year, an oracle declared that "Mikukurumitama" should be the deity's name, and the shrine was established to house this deity alongside four others.
=== Historical Records === The shrine may correspond to the "Kawachi Province Wani Shrine" mentioned in 850 CE, where the deity's rank was raised to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, it also served as a clan shrine for the {{ill|Kusunoki family|ja|楠木氏}}, who venerated the shrine as their ancestral deity.
The shrine was burned down during the {{ill|Siege of Akasaka|ja|赤坂城の戦い}} in 1331 but was later rebuilt under the support of {{ill|Kusunoki Masashige|ja|楠木正成}}. In 1585, it was again destroyed during the {{ill|Kishū Campaign|ja|紀州征伐}} by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}. The current main shrine was reconstructed in 1660.
The shrine was designated as a gōsha in 1872 during the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}.
== Shrine Grounds == The shrine grounds include the following structures: * '''Main Shrine (Honden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Upper Worship Hall (Kami-haiden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Lower Worship Hall (Shimo-haiden):''' * '''Treasure Hall (Saigu-kura):''' * '''Gates and Torii:''' * '''Miyajinja Uramayama Kofun Group:''' Includes a keyhole-shaped kofun (58 m long) believed to be from the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Uramayama Kofun Explanation Board|publisher=Educational Board of Tondabayashi|accessdate=2020-08-28}}</ref>
== Subsidiary Shrines == * {{ill|Kumano Takahira Shrine|ja|熊野貴平神社}} * {{ill|Nagi Shrine|ja|南木神社}} – Dedicated to the Kusunoki family. * {{ill|Kuni no Ichi Shrine|ja|皇大神社}}
== Annual Festivals == The shrine's annual festival is held on the third Friday of October.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine}}
== External Links == * [https://osaka-info.jp/page/migukurumitamajinja Migukurumitama Shrine | OSAKA-INFO]

The shrine is also selected as one of the {{ill|100 Green Selections of Osaka|ja|大阪みどりの百選}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/midorikikaku/toshiryokka/hyakusenn.html|title=Osaka 100 Green Selections|publisher=Osaka Prefecture|accessdate=2016-12-23}}</ref> It is also referred to as the "Shimono Suibun Shrine" (下水分社) and, along with {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine}} (the Kamisuibun Shrine in {{ill|Chihayaakasaka|ja|千早赤阪村}}) and {{ill|Nishikori Shrine|ja|錦織神社}} (in Tondabayashi), it is one of the "Three Mikumari Shrines of {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}."
== Enshrined Deities == * '''Primary Deity:''' Migukurumitama no Ōkami (美具久留御魂大神) – The {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒魂・和魂}} (rough spirit) of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}}. It is believed to represent a water deity, deriving its name from "Mikukurumitama" (Water Swimming Spirit). * '''Additional Deities:''' ** {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Amemikumari no Kami|ja|天水分神}} (Heavenly Mikumari Deity) ** {{ill|Mizuhanome|lt=Mizuhanome no Mikoto|ja|弥都波迺売命}} ** {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Kuni no Mikumari no Kami|ja|国水分神}} ** {{ill|Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseribime no Mikoto|ja|須勢理比売命}} ** {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶比売神}}
== History == === Foundation === According to tradition, the shrine was founded in the 10th year of Emperor Sujin's reign (88 BCE). A giant serpent frequently appeared in the area, leading the emperor to identify it as the aramitama of Ōkuninushi. He dispatched {{ill|Izumo Furune|ja|出雲振根}} to enshrine the deity, borrowing sacred objects from {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|杵築大社}}.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Place Name Dictionary|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1986}}</ref>
In Emperor Sujin's 62nd year, an oracle declared that "Mikukurumitama" should be the deity's name, and the shrine was established to house this deity alongside four others.
=== Historical Records === The shrine may correspond to the "Kawachi Province Wani Shrine" mentioned in 850 CE, where the deity's rank was raised to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, it also served as a clan shrine for the {{ill|Kusunoki family|ja|楠木氏}}, who venerated the shrine as their ancestral deity.
The shrine was burned down during the {{ill|Siege of Akasaka|ja|赤坂城の戦い}} in 1331 but was later rebuilt under the support of {{ill|Kusunoki Masashige|ja|楠木正成}}. In 1585, it was again destroyed during the {{ill|Kishū Campaign|ja|紀州征伐}} by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}. The current main shrine was reconstructed in 1660.
The shrine was designated as a gōsha in 1872 during the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}.
== Shrine Grounds == The shrine grounds include the following structures: * '''Main Shrine (Honden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Upper Worship Hall (Kami-haiden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Lower Worship Hall (Shimo-haiden):''' * '''Treasure Hall (Saigu-kura):''' * '''Gates and Torii:''' * '''Miyajinja Uramayama Kofun Group:''' Includes a keyhole-shaped kofun (58 m long) believed to be from the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Uramayama Kofun Explanation Board|publisher=Educational Board of Tondabayashi|accessdate=2020-08-28}}</ref>
== Subsidiary Shrines == * {{ill|Kumano Takahira Shrine|ja|熊野貴平神社}} * {{ill|Nagi Shrine|ja|南木神社}} – Dedicated to the Kusunoki family. * {{ill|Kuni no Ichi Shrine|ja|皇大神社}}
== Annual Festivals == The shrine's annual festival is held on the third Friday of October.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine}}
== External Links == * [https://osaka-info.jp/page/migukurumitamajinja Migukurumitama Shrine | OSAKA-INFO]

== History == === Foundation === According to tradition, the shrine was founded in the 10th year of Emperor Sujin's reign (88 BCE). A giant serpent frequently appeared in the area, leading the emperor to identify it as the aramitama of Ōkuninushi. He dispatched {{ill|Izumo Furune|ja|出雲振根}} to enshrine the deity, borrowing sacred objects from {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|杵築大社}}.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Place Name Dictionary|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1986}}</ref>
In Emperor Sujin's 62nd year, an oracle declared that "Mikukurumitama" should be the deity's name, and the shrine was established to house this deity alongside four others.
=== Historical Records === The shrine may correspond to the "Kawachi Province Wani Shrine" mentioned in 850 CE, where the deity's rank was raised to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, it also served as a clan shrine for the {{ill|Kusunoki family|ja|楠木氏}}, who venerated the shrine as their ancestral deity.
The shrine was burned down during the {{ill|Siege of Akasaka|ja|赤坂城の戦い}} in 1331 but was later rebuilt under the support of {{ill|Kusunoki Masashige|ja|楠木正成}}. In 1585, it was again destroyed during the {{ill|Kishū Campaign|ja|紀州征伐}} by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}. The current main shrine was reconstructed in 1660.
The shrine was designated as a gōsha in 1872 during the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}.
== Shrine Grounds == The shrine grounds include the following structures: * '''Main Shrine (Honden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Upper Worship Hall (Kami-haiden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Lower Worship Hall (Shimo-haiden):''' * '''Treasure Hall (Saigu-kura):''' * '''Gates and Torii:''' * '''Miyajinja Uramayama Kofun Group:''' Includes a keyhole-shaped kofun (58 m long) believed to be from the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Uramayama Kofun Explanation Board|publisher=Educational Board of Tondabayashi|accessdate=2020-08-28}}</ref>
== Subsidiary Shrines == * {{ill|Kumano Takahira Shrine|ja|熊野貴平神社}} * {{ill|Nagi Shrine|ja|南木神社}} – Dedicated to the Kusunoki family. * {{ill|Kuni no Ichi Shrine|ja|皇大神社}}
== Annual Festivals == The shrine's annual festival is held on the third Friday of October.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine}}
== External Links == * [https://osaka-info.jp/page/migukurumitamajinja Migukurumitama Shrine | OSAKA-INFO]
{{Munakata Faith}}

'''Migukurumitama Shrine''' (美具久留御魂神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Tondabayashi|ja|富田林市|12=draft}}, [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is an {{ill|historic shrine|ja|式内社|12=draft}} and was formerly ranked as a {{ill|gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}} under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. The shrine is also selected as one of the {{ill|100 Green Selections of Osaka|ja|大阪みどりの百選|12=draft}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/midorikikaku/toshiryokka/hyakusenn.html|title=Osaka 100 Green Selections|publisher=Osaka Prefecture|accessdate=2016-12-23}}</ref> It is also referred to as the "Shimono Suibun Shrine" (下水分社) and, along with {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine|12=draft}} (the Kamisuibun Shrine in {{ill|Chihayaakasaka|ja|千早赤阪村|12=draft}}) and {{ill|Nishikori Shrine|ja|錦織神社|12=draft}} (in Tondabayashi), it is one of the "Three Mikumari Shrines of {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国|12=draft}}."
== Enshrined Deities == * '''Primary Deity:''' Migukurumitama no Ōkami (美具久留御魂大神) – The {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒魂・和魂|12=draft}} (rough spirit) of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=draft}}. It is believed to represent a water deity, deriving its name from "Mikukurumitama" (Water Swimming Spirit). * '''Additional Deities:''' ** {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Amemikumari no Kami|ja|天水分神|12=draft}} (Heavenly Mikumari Deity) ** {{ill|Mizuhanome|lt=Mizuhanome no Mikoto|ja|弥都波迺売命|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Kuni no Mikumari no Kami|ja|国水分神|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseribime no Mikoto|ja|須勢理比売命|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶比売神|12=draft}}

During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft}}, it also served as a clan shrine for the {{ill|Kusunoki family|ja|楠木氏|12=draft}}, who venerated the shrine as their ancestral deity.
The shrine was burned down during the {{ill|Siege of Akasaka|ja|赤坂城の戦い|12=draft}} in 1331 but was later rebuilt under the support of {{ill|Kusunoki Masashige|ja|楠木正成|12=draft}}. The current main shrine was reconstructed in 1660.

== Subsidiary Shrines == * {{ill|Kumano Takahira Shrine|ja|熊野貴平神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nagi Shrine|ja|南木神社|12=draft}} – Dedicated to the Kusunoki family. * {{ill|Kuni no Ichi Shrine|ja|皇大神社|12=draft}}
== Annual Festivals == The shrine's annual festival is held on the third Friday of October.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine|12=draft}}
== External Links == * [https://osaka-info.jp/page/migukurumitamajinja Migukurumitama Shrine | OSAKA-INFO]

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Migukurumitama Shrine''' (美具久留御魂神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Tondabayashi|ja|富田林市|12=draft}}, [[Osaka Prefecture]]. It is an {{ill|historic shrine|ja|式内社|12=draft}} and was formerly ranked as a {{ill|gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}} under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. The shrine is also selected as one of the {{ill|100 Green Selections of Osaka|ja|大阪みどりの百選|12=draft}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/midorikikaku/toshiryokka/hyakusenn.html|title=Osaka 100 Green Selections|publisher=Osaka Prefecture|accessdate=2016-12-23}}</ref> It is also referred to as the "Shimono Suibun Shrine" (下水分社) and, along with {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine|12=draft}} (the Kamisuibun Shrine in {{ill|Chihayaakasaka|ja|千早赤阪村|12=draft}}) and {{ill|Nishikori Shrine|ja|錦織神社|12=draft}} (in Tondabayashi), it is one of the "Three Mikumari Shrines of {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国|12=draft}}."
== Enshrined Deities == * '''Primary Deity:''' Migukurumitama no Ōkami (美具久留御魂大神) – The {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒魂・和魂|12=draft}} (rough spirit) of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=draft}}. It is believed to represent a water deity, deriving its name from "Mikukurumitama" (Water Swimming Spirit). * '''Additional Deities:''' ** {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Amemikumari no Kami|ja|天水分神|12=draft}} (Heavenly Mikumari Deity) ** {{ill|Mizuhanome|lt=Mizuhanome no Mikoto|ja|弥都波迺売命|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Kuni no Mikumari no Kami|ja|国水分神|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseribime no Mikoto|ja|須勢理比売命|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶比売神|12=draft}}
== History == === Foundation === According to tradition, the shrine was founded in the 10th year of Emperor Sujin's reign (88 BCE). A giant serpent frequently appeared in the area, leading the emperor to identify it as the aramitama of Ōkuninushi. He dispatched {{ill|Izumo Furune|ja|出雲振根|12=draft}} to enshrine the deity, borrowing sacred objects from {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|杵築大社|12=draft}}.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Place Name Dictionary|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1986}}</ref>
In Emperor Sujin's 62nd year, an oracle declared that "Mikukurumitama" should be the deity's name, and the shrine was established to house this deity alongside four others.
=== Historical Records === The shrine may correspond to the "Kawachi Province Wani Shrine" mentioned in 850 CE, where the deity's rank was raised to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=draft}}. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft}}, it also served as a clan shrine for the {{ill|Kusunoki family|ja|楠木氏|12=draft}}, who venerated the shrine as their ancestral deity.
The shrine was burned down during the {{ill|Siege of Akasaka|ja|赤坂城の戦い|12=draft}} in 1331 but was later rebuilt under the support of {{ill|Kusunoki Masashige|ja|楠木正成|12=draft}}. In 1585, it was again destroyed during the {{ill|Kishū Campaign|ja|紀州征伐|12=draft}} by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|12=draft}}. The current main shrine was reconstructed in 1660.
The shrine was designated as a gōsha in 1872 during the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代|12=draft}}.
== Shrine Grounds == The shrine grounds include the following structures: * '''Main Shrine (Honden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Upper Worship Hall (Kami-haiden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Lower Worship Hall (Shimo-haiden):''' * '''Treasure Hall (Saigu-kura):''' * '''Gates and Torii:''' * '''Miyajinja Uramayama Kofun Group:''' Includes a keyhole-shaped kofun (58 m long) believed to be from the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Uramayama Kofun Explanation Board|publisher=Educational Board of Tondabayashi|accessdate=2020-08-28}}</ref>
== Subsidiary Shrines == * {{ill|Kumano Takahira Shrine|ja|熊野貴平神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nagi Shrine|ja|南木神社|12=draft}} – Dedicated to the Kusunoki family. * {{ill|Kuni no Ichi Shrine|ja|皇大神社|12=draft}}
== Annual Festivals == The shrine's annual festival is held on the third Friday of October.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine|12=draft}}
== External Links == * [https://osaka-info.jp/page/migukurumitamajinja Migukurumitama Shrine | OSAKA-INFO]
{{Munakata Faith}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Migukurumitama-jinja]][[ja:美具久留御魂神社]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Migukurumitama jinja shimohaiden.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Tondabayashi|qid=Q490928}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.51797222222222|N|135.60141666666667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11608777]] Wikidata name: Migukurumitama-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Migukurumitama Shrine''' (美具久留御魂神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tondabayashi|Tondabayashi]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. It is an {{ill|historic shrine|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/historic shrine|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. The shrine is also selected as one of the {{ill|100 Green Selections of Osaka|ja|大阪みどりの百選|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Green Selections of Osaka|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/midorikikaku/toshiryokka/hyakusenn.html|title=Osaka 100 Green Selections|publisher=Osaka Prefecture|accessdate=2016-12-23}}</ref> It is also referred to as the "Shimono Suibun Shrine" (下水分社) and, along with {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuta Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Mikumari Shrine|qq=}} (the Kamisuibun Shrine in [[:en:Chihayaakasaka|Chihayaakasaka]]) and {{ill|Nishikori Shrine|ja|錦織神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikori Shrine|qq=}} (in Tondabayashi), it is one of the "Three Mikumari Shrines of [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]]."
== Enshrined Deities == * '''Primary Deity:''' Migukurumitama no Ōkami (美具久留御魂大神) – The [[:en:Mitama|Aramitama]] (rough spirit) of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. It is believed to represent a water deity, deriving its name from "Mikukurumitama" (Water Swimming Spirit). * '''Additional Deities:''' ** {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Amemikumari no Kami|ja|天水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}} (Heavenly Mikumari Deity) ** {{ill|Mizuhanome|lt=Mizuhanome no Mikoto|ja|弥都波迺売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanome|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Kuni no Mikumari no Kami|ja|国水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}} ** {{ill|Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseribime no Mikoto|ja|須勢理比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suseri-Hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶比売神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== History == === Foundation === According to tradition, the shrine was founded in the 10th year of Emperor Sujin's reign (88 BCE). A giant serpent frequently appeared in the area, leading the emperor to identify it as the aramitama of Ōkuninushi. He dispatched {{ill|Izumo no Furune|ja|出雲振根|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Furune|qq=}} to enshrine the deity, borrowing sacred objects from {{ill|Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo Taisha|ja|杵築大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Taisha|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Place Name Dictionary|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1986}}</ref>
In Emperor Sujin's 62nd year, an oracle declared that "Mikukurumitama" should be the deity's name, and the shrine was established to house this deity alongside four others.
=== Historical Records === The shrine may correspond to the "Kawachi Province Wani Shrine" mentioned in 850 CE, where the deity's Rank was raised to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], it also served as a clan shrine for the {{ill|Kusunoki family|ja|楠木氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusunoki family|qq=|1=Kusunoki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541208}}, who venerated the shrine as their ancestral deity.
The shrine was burned down during the [[:en:Siege of Akasaka|Siege of Akasaka]] in 1331 but was later rebuilt under the support of [[:en:Kusunoki Masashige|Kusunoki Masashige]]. In 1585, it was again destroyed during the {{ill|Kishū Campaign|ja|紀州征伐|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishū Campaign|qq=|1=Battle of Kishuu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10855721}} by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. The current main shrine was reconstructed in 1660.
The shrine was designated as a gōsha in 1872 during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]].
== Shrine Grounds == The shrine grounds include the following structures: * '''Main Shrine (Honden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Upper Worship Hall (Kami-haiden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Lower Worship Hall (Shimo-haiden):''' * '''Treasure Hall (Saigu-kura):''' * '''Gates and Torii:''' * '''Miyajinja Uramayama Kofun Group:''' Includes a keyhole-shaped kofun (58 m long) believed to be from the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=Uramayama Kofun Explanation Board|publisher=Educational Board of Tondabayashi|accessdate=2020-08-28}}</ref>
== Subsidiary Shrines == * {{ill|Kumano Takahira Shrine|ja|熊野貴平神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Takahira Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Nagi Shrine|ja|南木神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagi Shrine|qq=|1=Nagi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22119792}} – Dedicated to the Kusunoki family. * {{ill|Kuni no Ichi Shrine|ja|皇大神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Ichi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神明神社|1=Shinmei shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590310}}
== Annual Festivals == The shrine's annual festival is held on the third Friday of October.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuta Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Mikumari Shrine|qq=}}
== External Links == * [https://osaka-info.jp/page/migukurumitamajinja Migukurumitama Shrine | OSAKA-INFO]

In 1585, it was again destroyed during the {{ill|Kishū Campaign|ja|紀州征伐|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishū Campaign|qq=|1=Battle of Kishuu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10855721}} by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. The current main shrine was reconstructed in 1660.
The shrine was designated as a gōsha in 1872 during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]].
== Shrine Grounds == The shrine grounds include the following structures: * '''Main Shrine (Honden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Upper Worship Hall (Kami-haiden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Lower Worship Hall (Shimo-haiden):''' * '''Treasure Hall (Saigu-kura):''' * '''Gates and Torii:''' * '''Miyajinja Uramayama Kofun Group:''' Includes a keyhole-shaped kofun (58 m long) believed to be from the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=Uramayama Kofun Explanation Board|publisher=Educational Board of Tondabayashi|accessdate=2020-08-28}}</ref>
== Subsidiary Shrines == * {{ill|Kumano Takahira Shrine|ja|熊野貴平神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Takahira Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Nagi Shrine|ja|南木神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagi Shrine|qq=|1=Nagi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22119792}} – Dedicated to the Kusunoki family. * {{ill|Kuni no Ichi Shrine|ja|皇大神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Ichi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神明神社|1=Shinmei shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590310}}
== Annual Festivals == The shrine's annual festival is held on the third Friday of October.
