The shrine is also selected as one of the {{ill|100 Green Selections of Osaka|ja|100 Greens of Ōsaka|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Green Selections of Osaka|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/midorikikaku/toshiryokka/hyakusenn.html|title=Osaka 100 Green Selections|publisher=Osaka Prefecture|accessdate=2016-12-23}}</ref> It is also referred to as the "Shimono Suibun Shrine" (下水分社) and, along with {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuta Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Mikumari Shrine|qq=}} (the Kamisuibun Shrine in [[:en:Chihayaakasaka|Chihayaakasaka]]) and {{ill|Nishikori Shrine|ja|錦織神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikori Shrine|qq=}} (in Tondabayashi), it is one of the "Three Mikumari Shrines of [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]]."
== Enshrined Deities == * '''Primary Deity:''' Migukurumitama no Ōkami (美具久留御魂大神) – The [[:en:Mitama|Aramitama]] (rough spirit) of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. It is believed to represent a water deity, deriving its name from "Mikukurumitama" (Water Swimming Spirit). * '''Additional Deities:''' ** {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Amemikumari no Kami|ja|天水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}} (Heavenly Mikumari Deity) ** {{ill|Mizuhanome|lt=Mizuhanome no Mikoto|ja|弥都波迺売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Kuni no Mikumari no Kami|ja|国水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}} ** {{ill|Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseribime no Mikoto|ja|須勢理比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suseri-Hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== History == === Foundation === According to tradition, the shrine was founded in the 10th year of Emperor Sujin's reign (88 BCE). A giant serpent frequently appeared in the area, leading the emperor to identify it as the aramitama of Ōkuninushi. He dispatched {{ill|Izumo no Furune|ja|出雲振根|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Furune|qq=}} to enshrine the deity, borrowing sacred objects from {{ill|Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo Taisha|ja|杵築大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Taisha|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Place Name Dictionary|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1986}}</ref>
In Emperor Sujin's 62nd year, an oracle declared that "Mikukurumitama" should be the deity's name, and the shrine was established to house this deity alongside four others.
=== Historical Records === The shrine may correspond to the "Kawachi Province Wani Shrine" mentioned in 850 CE, where the deity's Rank was raised to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], it also served as a clan shrine for the {{ill|Kusunoki family|ja|楠木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusunoki family|1=Kusunoki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541208}}, who venerated the shrine as their ancestral deity.
The shrine was burned down during the [[:en:Siege of Akasaka|Siege of Akasaka]] in 1331 but was later rebuilt under the support of [[:en:Kusunoki Masashige|Kusunoki Masashige]]. In 1585, it was again destroyed during the {{ill|Kishū Campaign|ja|紀州征伐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishū Campaign|1=Battle of Kishuu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10855721}} by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. The current main shrine was reconstructed in 1660.
The shrine was designated as a gōsha in 1872 during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]].
== Shrine Grounds == The shrine grounds include the following structures: * '''Main Shrine (Honden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Upper Worship Hall (Kami-haiden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Lower Worship Hall (Shimo-haiden):''' * '''Treasure Hall (Saigu-kura):''' * '''Gates and Torii:''' * '''Miyajinja Uramayama Kofun Group:''' Includes a keyhole-shaped kofun (58 m long) believed to be from the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=Uramayama Kofun Explanation Board|publisher=Educational Board of Tondabayashi|accessdate=2020-08-28}}</ref>
== Subsidiary Shrines == * {{ill|Kumano Takahira Shrine|ja|熊野貴平神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Takahira Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Nagi Shrine|ja|南木神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagi Shrine|1=Nagi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22119792}} – Dedicated to the Kusunoki family. * {{ill|Kuni no Ichi Shrine|ja|皇大神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Ichi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神明神社|1=Shinmei shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590310}}
== Annual Festivals == The shrine's annual festival is held on the third Friday of October.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuta Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Mikumari Shrine|qq=}}
== External Links == * [https://osaka-info.jp/page/migukurumitamajinja Migukurumitama Shrine | OSAKA-INFO]

=== Historical Records === The shrine may correspond to the "Kawachi Province Wani Shrine" mentioned in 850 CE, where the deity's Rank was raised to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], it also served as a clan shrine for the {{ill|Kusunoki family|ja|楠木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusunoki family|1=Kusunoki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541208}}, who venerated the shrine as their ancestral deity.
The shrine was burned down during the [[:en:Siege of Akasaka|Siege of Akasaka]] in 1331 but was later rebuilt under the support of [[:en:Kusunoki Masashige|Kusunoki Masashige]]. In 1585, it was again destroyed during the {{ill|Kishū Campaign|ja|紀州征伐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishū Campaign|1=Battle of Kishuu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10855721}} by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. The current main shrine was reconstructed in 1660.
The shrine was designated as a gōsha in 1872 during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]].
== Shrine Grounds == The shrine grounds include the following structures: * '''Main Shrine (Honden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Upper Worship Hall (Kami-haiden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Lower Worship Hall (Shimo-haiden):''' * '''Treasure Hall (Saigu-kura):''' * '''Gates and Torii:''' * '''Miyajinja Uramayama Kofun Group:''' Includes a keyhole-shaped kofun (58 m long) believed to be from the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=Uramayama Kofun Explanation Board|publisher=Educational Board of Tondabayashi|accessdate=2020-08-28}}</ref>
== Subsidiary Shrines == * {{ill|Kumano Takahira Shrine|ja|熊野貴平神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Takahira Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Nagi Shrine|ja|南木神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagi Shrine|1=Nagi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22119792}} – Dedicated to the Kusunoki family. * {{ill|Kuni no Ichi Shrine|ja|皇大神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Ichi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神明神社|1=Shinmei shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590310}}
== Annual Festivals == The shrine's annual festival is held on the third Friday of October.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuta Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Mikumari Shrine|qq=}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Migukurumitama-jinja]][[ja:美具久留御魂神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Migukurumitama-jinja]][[ja:美具久留御魂神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Migukurumitama-jinja]][[ja:美具久留御魂神社]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Migukurumitama jinja shimohaiden.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Tondabayashi|WD=Q490928}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.51797222222222|N|135.60141666666667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11608777]] Wikidata name: Migukurumitama-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Migukurumitama Shrine''' (美具久留御魂神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tondabayashi|Tondabayashi]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. It is an {{ill|historic shrine|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/historic shrine|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. The shrine is also selected as one of the {{ill|100 Green Selections of Osaka|ja|100 Greens of Ōsaka|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Green Selections of Osaka|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/midorikikaku/toshiryokka/hyakusenn.html|title=Osaka 100 Green Selections|publisher=Osaka Prefecture|accessdate=2016-12-23}}</ref> It is also referred to as the "Shimono Suibun Shrine" (下水分社) and, along with {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuta Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Mikumari Shrine|qq=}} (the Kamisuibun Shrine in [[:en:Chihayaakasaka|Chihayaakasaka]]) and {{ill|Nishikori Shrine|ja|錦織神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikori Shrine|qq=}} (in Tondabayashi), it is one of the "Three Mikumari Shrines of [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]]."
== Enshrined Deities == * '''Primary Deity:''' Migukurumitama no Ōkami (美具久留御魂大神) – The [[:en:Mitama|Aramitama]] (rough spirit) of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. It is believed to represent a water deity, deriving its name from "Mikukurumitama" (Water Swimming Spirit). * '''Additional Deities:''' ** {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Amemikumari no Kami|ja|天水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}} (Heavenly Mikumari Deity) ** {{ill|Mizuhanome|lt=Mizuhanome no Mikoto|ja|弥都波迺売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Kuni no Mikumari no Kami|ja|国水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}} ** {{ill|Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseribime no Mikoto|ja|須勢理比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suseri-Hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== History == === Foundation === According to tradition, the shrine was founded in the 10th year of Emperor Sujin's reign (88 BCE). A giant serpent frequently appeared in the area, leading the emperor to identify it as the aramitama of Ōkuninushi. He dispatched {{ill|Izumo no Furune|ja|出雲振根|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Furune|qq=}} to enshrine the deity, borrowing sacred objects from {{ill|Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo Taisha|ja|杵築大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Taisha|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Place Name Dictionary|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1986}}</ref>
In Emperor Sujin's 62nd year, an oracle declared that "Mikukurumitama" should be the deity's name, and the shrine was established to house this deity alongside four others.
=== Historical Records === The shrine may correspond to the "Kawachi Province Wani Shrine" mentioned in 850 CE, where the deity's Rank was raised to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], it also served as a clan shrine for the {{ill|Kusunoki family|ja|楠木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusunoki family|1=Kusunoki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541208}}, who venerated the shrine as their ancestral deity.
The shrine was burned down during the [[:en:Siege of Akasaka|Siege of Akasaka]] in 1331 but was later rebuilt under the support of [[:en:Kusunoki Masashige|Kusunoki Masashige]]. In 1585, it was again destroyed during the {{ill|Kishū Campaign|ja|紀州征伐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishū Campaign|1=Battle of Kishuu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10855721}} by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. The current main shrine was reconstructed in 1660.
The shrine was designated as a gōsha in 1872 during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]].
== Shrine Grounds == The shrine grounds include the following structures: * '''Main Shrine (Honden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Upper Worship Hall (Kami-haiden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Lower Worship Hall (Shimo-haiden):''' * '''Treasure Hall (Saigu-kura):''' * '''Gates and Torii:''' * '''Miyajinja Uramayama Kofun Group:''' Includes a keyhole-shaped kofun (58 m long) believed to be from the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=Uramayama Kofun Explanation Board|publisher=Educational Board of Tondabayashi|accessdate=2020-08-28}}</ref>
== Subsidiary Shrines == * {{ill|Kumano Takahira Shrine|ja|熊野貴平神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Takahira Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Nagi Shrine|ja|南木神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagi Shrine|1=Nagi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22119792}} – Dedicated to the Kusunoki family. * {{ill|Kuni no Ichi Shrine|ja|皇大神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Ichi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神明神社|1=Shinmei shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590310}}
== Annual Festivals == The shrine's annual festival is held on the third Friday of October.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuta Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Mikumari Shrine|qq=}}
== External Links == * [https://osaka-info.jp/page/migukurumitamajinja Migukurumitama Shrine | OSAKA-INFO]

It is believed to represent a water deity, deriving its name from "Mikukurumitama" (Water Swimming Spirit). * '''Additional Deities:''' ** {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Amemikumari no Kami|ja|天水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}} (Heavenly Mikumari Deity) ** {{ill|Mizuhanome|lt=Mizuhanome no Mikoto|ja|弥都波迺売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Kuni no Mikumari no Kami|ja|国水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}} ** {{ill|Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseribime no Mikoto|ja|須勢理比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suseri-Hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Migukurumitama-jinja]][[ja:美具久留御魂神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Migukurumitama-jinja]][[ja:美具久留御魂神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Migukurumitama-jinja]][[ja:美具久留御魂神社]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Migukurumitama jinja shimohaiden.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Tondabayashi|WD=Q490928}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.51797222222222|N|135.60141666666667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11608777]] Wikidata name: Migukurumitama-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Migukurumitama Shrine''' (美具久留御魂神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tondabayashi|Tondabayashi]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. It is an {{ill|historic shrine|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/historic shrine|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. The shrine is also selected as one of the {{ill|100 Green Selections of Osaka|ja|100 Greens of Ōsaka|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Green Selections of Osaka|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/midorikikaku/toshiryokka/hyakusenn.html|title=Osaka 100 Green Selections|publisher=Osaka Prefecture|accessdate=2016-12-23}}</ref> It is also referred to as the "Shimono Suibun Shrine" (下水分社) and, along with {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuta Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Mikumari Shrine|qq=}} (the Kamisuibun Shrine in [[:en:Chihayaakasaka|Chihayaakasaka]]) and {{ill|Nishikori Shrine|ja|錦織神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikori Shrine|qq=}} (in Tondabayashi), it is one of the "Three Mikumari Shrines of [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]]."
== Enshrined Deities == * '''Primary Deity:''' Migukurumitama no Ōkami (美具久留御魂大神) – The [[:en:Mitama|Aramitama]] (rough spirit) of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. It is believed to represent a water deity, deriving its name from "Mikukurumitama" (Water Swimming Spirit). * '''Additional Deities:''' ** {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Amemikumari no Kami|ja|天水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}} (Heavenly Mikumari Deity) ** {{ill|Mizuhanome|lt=Mizuhanome no Mikoto|ja|弥都波迺売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Kuni no Mikumari no Kami|ja|国水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}} ** {{ill|Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseribime no Mikoto|ja|須勢理比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suseri-Hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== History == === Foundation === According to tradition, the shrine was founded in the 10th year of Emperor Sujin's reign (88 BCE). A giant serpent frequently appeared in the area, leading the emperor to identify it as the aramitama of Ōkuninushi. He dispatched {{ill|Izumo no Furune|ja|出雲振根|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Furune|qq=}} to enshrine the deity, borrowing sacred objects from {{ill|Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo Taisha|ja|杵築大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Taisha|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Place Name Dictionary|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1986}}</ref>
In Emperor Sujin's 62nd year, an oracle declared that "Mikukurumitama" should be the deity's name, and the shrine was established to house this deity alongside four others.
=== Historical Records === The shrine may correspond to the "Kawachi Province Wani Shrine" mentioned in 850 CE, where the deity's Rank was raised to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], it also served as a clan shrine for the {{ill|Kusunoki family|ja|楠木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusunoki family|1=Kusunoki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541208}}, who venerated the shrine as their ancestral deity.
The shrine was burned down during the [[:en:Siege of Akasaka|Siege of Akasaka]] in 1331 but was later rebuilt under the support of [[:en:Kusunoki Masashige|Kusunoki Masashige]]. In 1585, it was again destroyed during the {{ill|Kishū Campaign|ja|紀州征伐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishū Campaign|1=Battle of Kishuu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10855721}} by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. The current main shrine was reconstructed in 1660.
The shrine was designated as a gōsha in 1872 during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]].
== Shrine Grounds == The shrine grounds include the following structures: * '''Main Shrine (Honden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Upper Worship Hall (Kami-haiden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Lower Worship Hall (Shimo-haiden):''' * '''Treasure Hall (Saigu-kura):''' * '''Gates and Torii:''' * '''Miyajinja Uramayama Kofun Group:''' Includes a keyhole-shaped kofun (58 m long) believed to be from the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=Uramayama Kofun Explanation Board|publisher=Educational Board of Tondabayashi|accessdate=2020-08-28}}</ref>
== Subsidiary Shrines == * {{ill|Kumano Takahira Shrine|ja|熊野貴平神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Takahira Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Nagi Shrine|ja|南木神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagi Shrine|1=Nagi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22119792}} – Dedicated to the Kusunoki family. * {{ill|Kuni no Ichi Shrine|ja|皇大神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Ichi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神明神社|1=Shinmei shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590310}}
== Annual Festivals == The shrine's annual festival is held on the third Friday of October.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuta Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Mikumari Shrine|qq=}}

The shrine is also selected as one of the {{ill|100 Green Selections of Osaka|ja|100 Greens of Ōsaka|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Green Selections of Osaka|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/midorikikaku/toshiryokka/hyakusenn.html|title=Osaka 100 Green Selections|publisher=Osaka Prefecture|accessdate=2016-12-23}}</ref> It is also referred to as the "Shimono Suibun Shrine" (下水分社) and, along with {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuta Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Mikumari Shrine|qq=}} (the Kamisuibun Shrine in [[:en:Chihayaakasaka|Chihayaakasaka]]) and {{ill|Nishikori Shrine|ja|錦織神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikori Shrine|qq=}} (in Tondabayashi), it is one of the "Three Mikumari Shrines of [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]]."

It is believed to represent a water deity, deriving its name from "Mikukurumitama" (Water Swimming Spirit). * '''Additional Deities:''' ** {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Amemikumari no Kami|ja|天水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}} (Heavenly Mikumari Deity) ** {{ill|Mizuhanome|lt=Mizuhanome no Mikoto|ja|弥都波迺売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Kuni no Mikumari no Kami|ja|国水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}} ** {{ill|Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseribime no Mikoto|ja|須勢理比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suseri-Hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== History == === Foundation === According to tradition, the shrine was founded in the 10th year of Emperor Sujin's reign (88 BCE). A giant serpent frequently appeared in the area, leading the emperor to identify it as the aramitama of Ōkuninushi. He dispatched {{ill|Izumo no Furune|ja|出雲振根|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Furune|qq=}} to enshrine the deity, borrowing sacred objects from {{ill|Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo Taisha|ja|杵築大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Taisha|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Place Name Dictionary|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1986}}</ref>
In Emperor Sujin's 62nd year, an oracle declared that "Mikukurumitama" should be the deity's name, and the shrine was established to house this deity alongside four others.
=== Historical Records === The shrine may correspond to the "Kawachi Province Wani Shrine" mentioned in 850 CE, where the deity's Rank was raised to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], it also served as a clan shrine for the {{ill|Kusunoki family|ja|楠木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusunoki family|1=Kusunoki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541208}}, who venerated the shrine as their ancestral deity.
The shrine was burned down during the [[:en:Siege of Akasaka|Siege of Akasaka]] in 1331 but was later rebuilt under the support of [[:en:Kusunoki Masashige|Kusunoki Masashige]]. In 1585, it was again destroyed during the {{ill|Kishū Campaign|ja|紀州征伐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishū Campaign|1=Battle of Kishuu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10855721}} by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. The current main shrine was reconstructed in 1660.
The shrine was designated as a gōsha in 1872 during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]].
== Shrine Grounds == The shrine grounds include the following structures: * '''Main Shrine (Honden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Upper Worship Hall (Kami-haiden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Lower Worship Hall (Shimo-haiden):''' * '''Treasure Hall (Saigu-kura):''' * '''Gates and Torii:''' * '''Miyajinja Uramayama Kofun Group:''' Includes a keyhole-shaped kofun (58 m long) believed to be from the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=Uramayama Kofun Explanation Board|publisher=Educational Board of Tondabayashi|accessdate=2020-08-28}}</ref>
== Subsidiary Shrines == * {{ill|Kumano Takahira Shrine|ja|熊野貴平神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Takahira Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Nagi Shrine|ja|南木神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagi Shrine|1=Nagi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22119792}} – Dedicated to the Kusunoki family. * {{ill|Kuni no Ichi Shrine|ja|皇大神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Ichi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神明神社|1=Shinmei shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590310}}
== Annual Festivals == The shrine's annual festival is held on the third Friday of October.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuta Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Mikumari Shrine|qq=}}
== External Links == * [https://osaka-info.jp/page/migukurumitamajinja Migukurumitama Shrine | OSAKA-INFO]

It is believed to represent a water deity, deriving its name from "Mikukurumitama" (Water Swimming Spirit). * '''Additional Deities:''' ** {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Amemikumari no Kami|ja|天水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}} (Heavenly Mikumari Deity) ** {{ill|Mizuhanome|lt=Mizuhanome no Mikoto|ja|弥都波迺売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Kuni no Mikumari no Kami|ja|国水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}} ** {{ill|Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseribime no Mikoto|ja|須勢理比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suseri-Hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

He dispatched {{ill|Izumo no Furune|ja|出雲振根|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Furune|qq=}} to enshrine the deity, borrowing sacred objects from {{ill|Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo Taisha|ja|杵築大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Taisha|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Place Name Dictionary|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1986}}</ref>

== Subsidiary Shrines == * {{ill|Kumano Takahira Shrine|ja|熊野貴平神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Takahira Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Nagi Shrine|ja|南木神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagi Shrine|1=Nagi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22119792}} – Dedicated to the Kusunoki family. * {{ill|Kuni no Ichi Shrine|ja|皇大神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Ichi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神明神社|1=Shinmei shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590310}}

During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], it also served as a clan shrine for the {{ill|Kusunoki family|ja|楠木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusunoki family|1=Kusunoki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541208}}, who venerated the shrine as their ancestral deity.
The shrine was burned down during the [[:en:Siege of Akasaka|Siege of Akasaka]] in 1331 but was later rebuilt under the support of [[:en:Kusunoki Masashige|Kusunoki Masashige]]. In 1585, it was again destroyed during the {{ill|Kishū Campaign|ja|紀州征伐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishū Campaign|1=Battle of Kishuu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10855721}} by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. The current main shrine was reconstructed in 1660.
The shrine was designated as a gōsha in 1872 during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]].
== Shrine Grounds == The shrine grounds include the following structures: * '''Main Shrine (Honden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Upper Worship Hall (Kami-haiden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Lower Worship Hall (Shimo-haiden):''' * '''Treasure Hall (Saigu-kura):''' * '''Gates and Torii:''' * '''Miyajinja Uramayama Kofun Group:''' Includes a keyhole-shaped kofun (58 m long) believed to be from the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=Uramayama Kofun Explanation Board|publisher=Educational Board of Tondabayashi|accessdate=2020-08-28}}</ref>
== Subsidiary Shrines == * {{ill|Kumano Takahira Shrine|ja|熊野貴平神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Takahira Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Nagi Shrine|ja|南木神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagi Shrine|1=Nagi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22119792}} – Dedicated to the Kusunoki family. * {{ill|Kuni no Ichi Shrine|ja|皇大神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Ichi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神明神社|1=Shinmei shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590310}}
== Annual Festivals == The shrine's annual festival is held on the third Friday of October.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuta Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Mikumari Shrine|qq=}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata--> <!--interwikis from wikidata--> <!--interwikis from wikidata--> {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Migukurumitama jinja shimohaiden.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Tondabayashi|WD=Q490928}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.51797222222222|N|135.60141666666667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: Migukurumitama-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Migukurumitama Shrine''' (美具久留御魂神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tondabayashi|Tondabayashi]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. It is an {{ill|historic shrine|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/historic shrine|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. The shrine is also selected as one of the {{ill|100 Green Selections of Osaka|ja|100 Greens of Ōsaka|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Green Selections of Osaka|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/midorikikaku/toshiryokka/hyakusenn.html|title=Osaka 100 Green Selections|publisher=Osaka Prefecture|accessdate=2016-12-23}}</ref> It is also referred to as the "Shimono Suibun Shrine" (下水分社) and, along with {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuta Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Mikumari Shrine|qq=}} (the Kamisuibun Shrine in [[:en:Chihayaakasaka|Chihayaakasaka]]) and {{ill|Nishikori Shrine|ja|錦織神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikori Shrine|qq=}} (in Tondabayashi), it is one of the "Three Mikumari Shrines of [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]]."
== Enshrined Deities == * '''Primary Deity:''' Migukurumitama no Ōkami (美具久留御魂大神) – The [[:en:Mitama|Aramitama]] (rough spirit) of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. It is believed to represent a water deity, deriving its name from "Mikukurumitama" (Water Swimming Spirit). * '''Additional Deities:''' ** {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Amemikumari no Kami|ja|天水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}} (Heavenly Mikumari Deity) ** {{ill|Mizuhanome|lt=Mizuhanome no Mikoto|ja|弥都波迺売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Kuni no Mikumari no Kami|ja|国水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}} ** {{ill|Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseribime no Mikoto|ja|須勢理比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suseri-Hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== History == === Foundation === According to tradition, the shrine was founded in the 10th year of Emperor Sujin's reign (88 BCE). A giant serpent frequently appeared in the area, leading the emperor to identify it as the aramitama of Ōkuninushi. He dispatched {{ill|Izumo no Furune|ja|出雲振根|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Furune|qq=}} to enshrine the deity, borrowing sacred objects from {{ill|Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo Taisha|ja|杵築大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Taisha|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Place Name Dictionary|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1986}}</ref>
In Emperor Sujin's 62nd year, an oracle declared that "Mikukurumitama" should be the deity's name, and the shrine was established to house this deity alongside four others.
=== Historical Records === The shrine may correspond to the "Kawachi Province Wani Shrine" mentioned in 850 CE, where the deity's Rank was raised to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], it also served as a clan shrine for the {{ill|Kusunoki family|ja|楠木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusunoki family|1=Kusunoki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541208}}, who venerated the shrine as their ancestral deity.
The shrine was burned down during the [[:en:Siege of Akasaka|Siege of Akasaka]] in 1331 but was later rebuilt under the support of [[:en:Kusunoki Masashige|Kusunoki Masashige]]. In 1585, it was again destroyed during the {{ill|Kishū Campaign|ja|紀州征伐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishū Campaign|1=Battle of Kishuu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10855721}} by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. The current main shrine was reconstructed in 1660.
The shrine was designated as a gōsha in 1872 during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]].
== Shrine Grounds == The shrine grounds include the following structures: * '''Main Shrine (Honden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Upper Worship Hall (Kami-haiden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Lower Worship Hall (Shimo-haiden):''' * '''Treasure Hall (Saigu-kura):''' * '''Gates and Torii:''' * '''Miyajinja Uramayama Kofun Group:''' Includes a keyhole-shaped kofun (58 m long) believed to be from the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=Uramayama Kofun Explanation Board|publisher=Educational Board of Tondabayashi|accessdate=2020-08-28}}</ref>
== Subsidiary Shrines == * {{ill|Kumano Takahira Shrine|ja|熊野貴平神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Takahira Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Nagi Shrine|ja|南木神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagi Shrine|1=Nagi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22119792}} – Dedicated to the Kusunoki family. * {{ill|Kuni no Ichi Shrine|ja|皇大神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Ichi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神明神社|1=Shinmei shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590310}}
== Annual Festivals == The shrine's annual festival is held on the third Friday of October.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuta Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Mikumari Shrine|qq=}}
== External Links == * [https://osaka-info.jp/page/migukurumitamajinja Migukurumitama Shrine | OSAKA-INFO]

== Enshrined Deities == * '''Primary Deity:''' Migukurumitama no Ōkami (美具久留御魂大神) – The {{ill|Mitama|en|Mitama|WD=Q495568|lt=Aramitama}} (rough spirit) of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. It is believed to represent a water deity, deriving its name from "Mikukurumitama" (Water Swimming Spirit). * '''Additional Deities:''' ** {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Amemikumari no Kami|ja|天水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}} (Heavenly Mikumari Deity) ** {{ill|Mizuhanome|lt=Mizuhanome no Mikoto|ja|弥都波迺売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Kuni no Mikumari no Kami|ja|国水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}} ** {{ill|Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseribime no Mikoto|ja|須勢理比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suseri-Hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

=== Historical Records === The shrine may correspond to the "Kawachi Province Wani Shrine" mentioned in 850 CE, where the deity's Rank was raised to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. During the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, it also served as a clan shrine for the {{ill|Kusunoki family|ja|楠木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusunoki family|1=Kusunoki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541208}}, who venerated the shrine as their ancestral deity.

In 1585, it was again destroyed during the {{ill|Kishū Campaign|ja|紀州征伐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishū Campaign|1=Battle of Kishuu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10855721}} by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}. The current main shrine was reconstructed in 1660.
The shrine was designated as a gōsha in 1872 during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period}}.
== Shrine Grounds == The shrine grounds include the following structures: * '''Main Shrine (Honden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Upper Worship Hall (Kami-haiden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Lower Worship Hall (Shimo-haiden):''' * '''Treasure Hall (Saigu-kura):''' * '''Gates and Torii:''' * '''Miyajinja Uramayama Kofun Group:''' Includes a keyhole-shaped kofun (58 m long) believed to be from the early {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Uramayama Kofun Explanation Board|publisher=Educational Board of Tondabayashi|accessdate=2020-08-28}}</ref>
== Subsidiary Shrines == * {{ill|Kumano Takahira Shrine|ja|熊野貴平神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Takahira Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Nagi Shrine|ja|南木神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagi Shrine|1=Nagi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22119792}} – Dedicated to the Kusunoki family. * {{ill|Kuni no Ichi Shrine|ja|皇大神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Ichi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神明神社|1=Shinmei shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590310}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata--> <!--interwikis from wikidata--> <!--interwikis from wikidata--> {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Migukurumitama jinja shimohaiden.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Tondabayashi|WD=Q490928}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.51797222222222|N|135.60141666666667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: Migukurumitama-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Migukurumitama Shrine''' (美具久留御魂神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Tondabayashi|en|Tondabayashi|WD=Q490928|lt=Tondabayashi}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is an {{ill|historic shrine|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/historic shrine|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. The shrine is also selected as one of the {{ill|100 Green Selections of Osaka|ja|100 Greens of Ōsaka|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Green Selections of Osaka|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/midorikikaku/toshiryokka/hyakusenn.html|title=Osaka 100 Green Selections|publisher=Osaka Prefecture|accessdate=2016-12-23}}</ref> It is also referred to as the "Shimono Suibun Shrine" (下水分社) and, along with {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuta Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Mikumari Shrine|qq=}} (the Kamisuibun Shrine in {{ill|Chihayaakasaka|en|Chihayaakasaka|WD=Q1072574|lt=Chihayaakasaka}}) and {{ill|Nishikori Shrine|ja|錦織神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikori Shrine|qq=}} (in Tondabayashi), it is one of the "Three Mikumari Shrines of {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province}}."
== Enshrined Deities == * '''Primary Deity:''' Migukurumitama no Ōkami (美具久留御魂大神) – The {{ill|Mitama|en|Mitama|WD=Q495568|lt=Aramitama}} (rough spirit) of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. It is believed to represent a water deity, deriving its name from "Mikukurumitama" (Water Swimming Spirit). * '''Additional Deities:''' ** {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Amemikumari no Kami|ja|天水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}} (Heavenly Mikumari Deity) ** {{ill|Mizuhanome|lt=Mizuhanome no Mikoto|ja|弥都波迺売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Kuni no Mikumari no Kami|ja|国水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}} ** {{ill|Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseribime no Mikoto|ja|須勢理比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suseri-Hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== History == === Foundation === According to tradition, the shrine was founded in the 10th year of Emperor Sujin's reign (88 BCE). A giant serpent frequently appeared in the area, leading the emperor to identify it as the aramitama of Ōkuninushi. He dispatched {{ill|Izumo no Furune|ja|出雲振根|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Furune|qq=}} to enshrine the deity, borrowing sacred objects from {{ill|Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo Taisha|ja|杵築大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Taisha|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Place Name Dictionary|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1986}}</ref>
In Emperor Sujin's 62nd year, an oracle declared that "Mikukurumitama" should be the deity's name, and the shrine was established to house this deity alongside four others.
=== Historical Records === The shrine may correspond to the "Kawachi Province Wani Shrine" mentioned in 850 CE, where the deity's Rank was raised to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. During the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, it also served as a clan shrine for the {{ill|Kusunoki family|ja|楠木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusunoki family|1=Kusunoki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541208}}, who venerated the shrine as their ancestral deity.
The shrine was burned down during the {{ill|Siege of Akasaka|en|Siege of Akasaka|WD=Q7509791|lt=Siege of Akasaka}} in 1331 but was later rebuilt under the support of {{ill|Kusunoki Masashige|en|Kusunoki Masashige|WD=Q382234|lt=Kusunoki Masashige}}. In 1585, it was again destroyed during the {{ill|Kishū Campaign|ja|紀州征伐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishū Campaign|1=Battle of Kishuu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10855721}} by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}. The current main shrine was reconstructed in 1660.
The shrine was designated as a gōsha in 1872 during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period}}.
== Shrine Grounds == The shrine grounds include the following structures: * '''Main Shrine (Honden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Upper Worship Hall (Kami-haiden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Lower Worship Hall (Shimo-haiden):''' * '''Treasure Hall (Saigu-kura):''' * '''Gates and Torii:''' * '''Miyajinja Uramayama Kofun Group:''' Includes a keyhole-shaped kofun (58 m long) believed to be from the early {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Uramayama Kofun Explanation Board|publisher=Educational Board of Tondabayashi|accessdate=2020-08-28}}</ref>
== Subsidiary Shrines == * {{ill|Kumano Takahira Shrine|ja|熊野貴平神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Takahira Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Nagi Shrine|ja|南木神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagi Shrine|1=Nagi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22119792}} – Dedicated to the Kusunoki family. * {{ill|Kuni no Ichi Shrine|ja|皇大神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Ichi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神明神社|1=Shinmei shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590310}}
== Annual Festivals == The shrine's annual festival is held on the third Friday of October.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuta Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Mikumari Shrine|qq=}}
== External Links == * [https://osaka-info.jp/page/migukurumitamajinja Migukurumitama Shrine | OSAKA-INFO]

<!--interwikis from wikidata--> <!--interwikis from wikidata--> <!--interwikis from wikidata--> {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Migukurumitama jinja shimohaiden.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Tondabayashi|WD=Q490928}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.51797222222222|N|135.60141666666667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: Migukurumitama-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Migukurumitama Shrine''' (美具久留御魂神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Tondabayashi|en|Tondabayashi|WD=Q490928|lt=Tondabayashi}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is an {{ill|historic shrine|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/historic shrine|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. The shrine is also selected as one of the {{ill|100 Green Selections of Osaka|ja|100 Greens of Ōsaka|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Green Selections of Osaka|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/midorikikaku/toshiryokka/hyakusenn.html|title=Osaka 100 Green Selections|publisher=Osaka Prefecture|accessdate=2016-12-23}}</ref> It is also referred to as the "Shimono Suibun Shrine" (下水分社) and, along with {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuta Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Mikumari Shrine|qq=}} (the Kamisuibun Shrine in {{ill|Chihayaakasaka|en|Chihayaakasaka|WD=Q1072574|lt=Chihayaakasaka}}) and {{ill|Nishikori Shrine|ja|錦織神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikori Shrine|qq=}} (in Tondabayashi), it is one of the "Three Mikumari Shrines of {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province}}."
== Enshrined Deities == * '''Primary Deity:''' Migukurumitama no Ōkami (美具久留御魂大神) – The {{ill|Mitama|en|Mitama|WD=Q495568|lt=Aramitama}} (rough spirit) of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. It is believed to represent a water deity, deriving its name from "Mikukurumitama" (Water Swimming Spirit). * '''Additional Deities:''' ** {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Amemikumari no Kami|ja|天水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}} (Heavenly Mikumari Deity) ** {{ill|Mizuhanome|lt=Mizuhanome no Mikoto|ja|弥都波迺売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Kuni no Mikumari no Kami|ja|国水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}} ** {{ill|Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseribime no Mikoto|ja|須勢理比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suseri-Hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== History == === Foundation === According to tradition, the shrine was founded in the 10th year of Emperor Sujin's reign (88 BCE). A giant serpent frequently appeared in the area, leading the emperor to identify it as the aramitama of Ōkuninushi. He dispatched {{ill|Izumo no Furune|ja|出雲振根|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Furune|qq=}} to enshrine the deity, borrowing sacred objects from {{ill|Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo Taisha|ja|杵築大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Taisha|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Place Name Dictionary|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1986}}</ref>
In Emperor Sujin's 62nd year, an oracle declared that "Mikukurumitama" should be the deity's name, and the shrine was established to house this deity alongside four others.
=== Historical Records === The shrine may correspond to the "Kawachi Province Wani Shrine" mentioned in 850 CE, where the deity's Rank was raised to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. During the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, it also served as a clan shrine for the {{ill|Kusunoki family|ja|楠木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusunoki family|1=Kusunoki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541208}}, who venerated the shrine as their ancestral deity.
The shrine was burned down during the {{ill|Siege of Akasaka|en|Siege of Akasaka|WD=Q7509791|lt=Siege of Akasaka}} in 1331 but was later rebuilt under the support of {{ill|Kusunoki Masashige|en|Kusunoki Masashige|WD=Q382234|lt=Kusunoki Masashige}}. In 1585, it was again destroyed during the {{ill|Kishū Campaign|ja|紀州征伐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishū Campaign|1=Battle of Kishuu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10855721}} by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}. The current main shrine was reconstructed in 1660.
The shrine was designated as a gōsha in 1872 during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period}}.
== Shrine Grounds == The shrine grounds include the following structures: * '''Main Shrine (Honden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Upper Worship Hall (Kami-haiden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Lower Worship Hall (Shimo-haiden):''' * '''Treasure Hall (Saigu-kura):''' * '''Gates and Torii:''' * '''Miyajinja Uramayama Kofun Group:''' Includes a keyhole-shaped kofun (58 m long) believed to be from the early {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Uramayama Kofun Explanation Board|publisher=Educational Board of Tondabayashi|accessdate=2020-08-28}}</ref>
== Subsidiary Shrines == * {{ill|Kumano Takahira Shrine|ja|熊野貴平神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Takahira Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Nagi Shrine|ja|南木神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagi Shrine|1=Nagi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22119792}} – Dedicated to the Kusunoki family. * {{ill|Kuni no Ichi Shrine|ja|皇大神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Ichi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神明神社|1=Shinmei shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590310}}
== Annual Festivals == The shrine's annual festival is held on the third Friday of October.

<!--interwikis from wikidata--> <!--interwikis from wikidata--> <!--interwikis from wikidata--> {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Migukurumitama jinja shimohaiden.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Tondabayashi|WD=Q490928}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.51797222222222|N|135.60141666666667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: Migukurumitama-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Migukurumitama Shrine''' (美具久留御魂神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Tondabayashi|en|Tondabayashi|WD=Q490928|lt=Tondabayashi}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is an {{ill|historic shrine|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/historic shrine|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. The shrine is also selected as one of the {{ill|100 Green Selections of Osaka|ja|100 Greens of Ōsaka|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Green Selections of Osaka|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/midorikikaku/toshiryokka/hyakusenn.html|title=Osaka 100 Green Selections|publisher=Osaka Prefecture|accessdate=2016-12-23}}</ref> It is also referred to as the "Shimono Suibun Shrine" (下水分社) and, along with {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuta Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Mikumari Shrine|qq=}} (the Kamisuibun Shrine in {{ill|Chihayaakasaka|en|Chihayaakasaka|WD=Q1072574|lt=Chihayaakasaka}}) and {{ill|Nishikori Shrine|ja|錦織神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikori Shrine|qq=}} (in Tondabayashi), it is one of the "Three Mikumari Shrines of {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province}}."
== Enshrined Deities == * '''Primary Deity:''' Migukurumitama no Ōkami (美具久留御魂大神) – The {{ill|Mitama|en|Mitama|WD=Q495568|lt=Aramitama}} (rough spirit) of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. It is believed to represent a water deity, deriving its name from "Mikukurumitama" (Water Swimming Spirit). * '''Additional Deities:''' ** {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Amemikumari no Kami|ja|天水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}} (Heavenly Mikumari Deity) ** {{ill|Mizuhanome|lt=Mizuhanome no Mikoto|ja|弥都波迺売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Kuni no Mikumari no Kami|ja|国水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}} ** {{ill|Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseribime no Mikoto|ja|須勢理比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suseri-Hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== History == === Foundation === According to tradition, the shrine was founded in the 10th year of Emperor Sujin's reign (88 BCE). A giant serpent frequently appeared in the area, leading the emperor to identify it as the aramitama of Ōkuninushi. He dispatched {{ill|Izumo no Furune|ja|出雲振根|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Furune|qq=}} to enshrine the deity, borrowing sacred objects from {{ill|Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo Taisha|ja|杵築大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Taisha|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Place Name Dictionary|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1986}}</ref>
In Emperor Sujin's 62nd year, an oracle declared that "Mikukurumitama" should be the deity's name, and the shrine was established to house this deity alongside four others.
=== Historical Records === The shrine may correspond to the "Kawachi Province Wani Shrine" mentioned in 850 CE, where the deity's Rank was raised to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. During the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, it also served as a clan shrine for the {{ill|Kusunoki family|ja|楠木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusunoki family|1=Kusunoki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541208}}, who venerated the shrine as their ancestral deity.
The shrine was burned down during the {{ill|Siege of Akasaka|en|Siege of Akasaka|WD=Q7509791|lt=Siege of Akasaka}} in 1331 but was later rebuilt under the support of {{ill|Kusunoki Masashige|en|Kusunoki Masashige|WD=Q382234|lt=Kusunoki Masashige}}. In 1585, it was again destroyed during the {{ill|Kishū Campaign|ja|紀州征伐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishū Campaign|1=Battle of Kishuu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10855721}} by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}. The current main shrine was reconstructed in 1660.
The shrine was designated as a gōsha in 1872 during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period}}.
== Shrine Grounds == The shrine grounds include the following structures: * '''Main Shrine (Honden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Upper Worship Hall (Kami-haiden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Lower Worship Hall (Shimo-haiden):''' * '''Treasure Hall (Saigu-kura):''' * '''Gates and Torii:''' * '''Miyajinja Uramayama Kofun Group:''' Includes a keyhole-shaped kofun (58 m long) believed to be from the early {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Uramayama Kofun Explanation Board|publisher=Educational Board of Tondabayashi|accessdate=2020-08-28}}</ref>
== Subsidiary Shrines == * {{ill|Kumano Takahira Shrine|ja|熊野貴平神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Takahira Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Nagi Shrine|ja|南木神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagi Shrine|1=Nagi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22119792}} – Dedicated to the Kusunoki family. * {{ill|Kuni no Ichi Shrine|ja|皇大神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Ichi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神明神社|1=Shinmei shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590310}}
== Annual Festivals == The shrine's annual festival is held on the third Friday of October.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuta Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Mikumari Shrine|qq=}}
== External Links == * [https://osaka-info.jp/page/migukurumitamajinja Migukurumitama Shrine | OSAKA-INFO]

The shrine is also selected as one of the {{ill|100 Green Selections of Osaka|ja|100 Greens of Ōsaka|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Green Selections of Osaka|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/midorikikaku/toshiryokka/hyakusenn.html|title=Osaka 100 Green Selections|publisher=Osaka Prefecture|accessdate=2016-12-23}}</ref> It is also referred to as the "Shimono Suibun Shrine" (下水分社) and, along with {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuta Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Mikumari Shrine|qq=}} (the Kamisuibun Shrine in {{ill|Chihayaakasaka|en|Chihayaakasaka|WD=Q1072574|lt=Chihayaakasaka}}) and {{ill|Nishikori Shrine|ja|錦織神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikori Shrine|qq=}} (in Tondabayashi), it is one of the "Three Mikumari Shrines of {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province}}."
== Enshrined Deities == * '''Primary Deity:''' Migukurumitama no Ōkami (美具久留御魂大神) – The {{ill|Mitama|en|Mitama|WD=Q495568|lt=Aramitama}} (rough spirit) of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. It is believed to represent a water deity, deriving its name from "Mikukurumitama" (Water Swimming Spirit). * '''Additional Deities:''' ** {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Amemikumari no Kami|ja|天水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}} (Heavenly Mikumari Deity) ** {{ill|Mizuhanome|lt=Mizuhanome no Mikoto|ja|弥都波迺売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Kuni no Mikumari no Kami|ja|国水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}} ** {{ill|Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseribime no Mikoto|ja|須勢理比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suseri-Hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== History == === Foundation === According to tradition, the shrine was founded in the 10th year of Emperor Sujin's reign (88 BCE). A giant serpent frequently appeared in the area, leading the emperor to identify it as the aramitama of Ōkuninushi. He dispatched {{ill|Izumo no Furune|ja|出雲振根|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Furune|qq=}} to enshrine the deity, borrowing sacred objects from {{ill|Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo Taisha|ja|杵築大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Taisha|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Place Name Dictionary|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1986}}</ref>
In Emperor Sujin's 62nd year, an oracle declared that "Mikukurumitama" should be the deity's name, and the shrine was established to house this deity alongside four others.
=== Historical Records === The shrine may correspond to the "Kawachi Province Wani Shrine" mentioned in 850 CE, where the deity's Rank was raised to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. During the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, it also served as a clan shrine for the {{ill|Kusunoki family|ja|楠木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusunoki family|1=Kusunoki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541208}}, who venerated the shrine as their ancestral deity.
The shrine was burned down during the {{ill|Siege of Akasaka|en|Siege of Akasaka|WD=Q7509791|lt=Siege of Akasaka}} in 1331 but was later rebuilt under the support of {{ill|Kusunoki Masashige|en|Kusunoki Masashige|WD=Q382234|lt=Kusunoki Masashige}}. In 1585, it was again destroyed during the {{ill|Kishū Campaign|ja|紀州征伐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishū Campaign|1=Battle of Kishuu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10855721}} by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}. The current main shrine was reconstructed in 1660.
The shrine was designated as a gōsha in 1872 during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period}}.
== Shrine Grounds == The shrine grounds include the following structures: * '''Main Shrine (Honden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Upper Worship Hall (Kami-haiden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Lower Worship Hall (Shimo-haiden):''' * '''Treasure Hall (Saigu-kura):''' * '''Gates and Torii:''' * '''Miyajinja Uramayama Kofun Group:''' Includes a keyhole-shaped kofun (58 m long) believed to be from the early {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Uramayama Kofun Explanation Board|publisher=Educational Board of Tondabayashi|accessdate=2020-08-28}}</ref>
== Subsidiary Shrines == * {{ill|Kumano Takahira Shrine|ja|熊野貴平神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Takahira Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Nagi Shrine|ja|南木神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagi Shrine|1=Nagi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22119792}} – Dedicated to the Kusunoki family. * {{ill|Kuni no Ichi Shrine|ja|皇大神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Ichi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神明神社|1=Shinmei shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590310}}
== Annual Festivals == The shrine's annual festival is held on the third Friday of October.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuta Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Mikumari Shrine|qq=}}
== External Links == * [https://osaka-info.jp/page/migukurumitamajinja Migukurumitama Shrine | OSAKA-INFO]

In Emperor Sujin's 62nd year, an oracle declared that "Mikukurumitama" should be the deity's name, and the shrine was established to house this deity alongside four others.
=== Historical Records === The shrine may correspond to the "Kawachi Province Wani Shrine" mentioned in 850 CE, where the deity's Rank was raised to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. During the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, it also served as a clan shrine for the {{ill|Kusunoki family|ja|楠木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusunoki family|1=Kusunoki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541208}}, who venerated the shrine as their ancestral deity.
The shrine was burned down during the {{ill|Siege of Akasaka|en|Siege of Akasaka|WD=Q7509791|lt=Siege of Akasaka}} in 1331 but was later rebuilt under the support of {{ill|Kusunoki Masashige|en|Kusunoki Masashige|WD=Q382234|lt=Kusunoki Masashige}}. In 1585, it was again destroyed during the {{ill|Kishū Campaign|ja|紀州征伐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishū Campaign|1=Battle of Kishuu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10855721}} by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}. The current main shrine was reconstructed in 1660.
The shrine was designated as a gōsha in 1872 during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period}}.
== Shrine Grounds == The shrine grounds include the following structures: * '''Main Shrine (Honden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Upper Worship Hall (Kami-haiden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Lower Worship Hall (Shimo-haiden):''' * '''Treasure Hall (Saigu-kura):''' * '''Gates and Torii:''' * '''Miyajinja Uramayama Kofun Group:''' Includes a keyhole-shaped kofun (58 m long) believed to be from the early {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Uramayama Kofun Explanation Board|publisher=Educational Board of Tondabayashi|accessdate=2020-08-28}}</ref>
== Subsidiary Shrines == * {{ill|Kumano Takahira Shrine|ja|熊野貴平神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Takahira Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Nagi Shrine|ja|南木神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagi Shrine|1=Nagi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22119792}} – Dedicated to the Kusunoki family. * {{ill|Kuni no Ichi Shrine|ja|皇大神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Ichi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神明神社|1=Shinmei shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590310}}
== Annual Festivals == The shrine's annual festival is held on the third Friday of October.

The shrine is also selected as one of the {{ill|100 Green Selections of Osaka|ja|100 Greens of Ōsaka|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Green Selections of Osaka|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/midorikikaku/toshiryokka/hyakusenn.html|title=Osaka 100 Green Selections|publisher=Osaka Prefecture|accessdate=2016-12-23}}</ref> It is also referred to as the "Shimono Suibun Shrine" (下水分社) and, along with {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuta Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Mikumari Shrine|qq=}} (the Kamisuibun Shrine in {{ill|Chihayaakasaka|en|Chihayaakasaka|WD=Q1072574|lt=Chihayaakasaka}}) and {{ill|Nishikori Shrine|ja|錦織神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikori Shrine|qq=}} (in Tondabayashi), it is one of the "Three Mikumari Shrines of {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province}}."

== History == === Foundation === According to tradition, the shrine was founded in the 10th year of Emperor Sujin's reign (88 BCE). A giant serpent frequently appeared in the area, leading the emperor to identify it as the aramitama of Ōkuninushi. In Emperor Sujin's 62nd year, an oracle declared that "Mikukurumitama" should be the deity's name, and the shrine was established to house this deity alongside four others.

The shrine was burned down during the {{ill|Siege of Akasaka|en|Siege of Akasaka|WD=Q7509791|lt=Siege of Akasaka}} in 1331 but was later rebuilt under the support of {{ill|Kusunoki Masashige|en|Kusunoki Masashige|WD=Q382234|lt=Kusunoki Masashige}}. The current main shrine was reconstructed in 1660.
The shrine was designated as a gōsha in 1872 during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period}}.

Shrine DB |- | RONSHA || {{ill|Migukurumitama Shrine|lt=Migukurumitama Shrine|ja|美具久留御魂神社|commons|Category:Migukurumitama-jinja|WD=Q11608777}} || {{ill|Kanpei-sha|WD=Q135160338|lt=Kanpei|comment=categorized}} || — || — || — || {{coord|34.517986|135.601478|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=180880 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q11608777}}==Notes== <references group="lower-alpha"/> [[co:commons:Category:Migukurumitama-jinja]] [[da:d:Q11608777]] [[ja:美具久留御魂神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Migukurumitama-jinja]] [[Category:Buildings in Tondabayashi, Osaka]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tondabayashi, Osaka]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]]
