<!--interwikis from wikidata--> <!--interwikis from wikidata--> <!--interwikis from wikidata--> {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Migukurumitama jinja shimohaiden.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Tondabayashi|WD=Q490928}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.51797222222222|N|135.60141666666667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{ '''Migukurumitama Shrine''' (美具久留御魂神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Tondabayashi|en|Tondabayashi|WD=Q490928|lt=Tondabayashi}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is an {{ill|historic shrine|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/historic shrine|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. The shrine is also selected as one of the {{ill|100 Green Selections of Osaka|ja|100 Greens of Ōsaka|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Green Selections of Osaka|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/midorikikaku/toshiryokka/hyakusenn.html|title=Osaka 100 Green Selections|publisher=Osaka Prefecture|accessdate=2016-12-23}}</ref> It is also referred to as the "Shimono Suibun Shrine" (下水分社) and, along with {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuta Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Mikumari Shrine|qq=}} (the Kamisuibun Shrine in {{ill|Chihayaakasaka|en|Chihayaakasaka|WD=Q1072574|lt=Chihayaakasaka}}) and {{ill|Nishikori Shrine|ja|錦織神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikori Shrine|qq=}} (in Tondabayashi), it is one of the "Three Mikumari Shrines of {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province}}."
== Enshrined Deities == * '''Primary Deity:''' Migukurumitama no Ōkami (美具久留御魂大神) – The {{ill|Mitama|en|Mitama|WD=Q495568|lt=Aramitama}} (rough spirit) of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. It is believed to represent a water deity, deriving its name from "Mikukurumitama" (Water Swimming Spirit). * '''Additional Deities:''' ** {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Amemikumari no Kami|ja|天水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}} (Heavenly Mikumari Deity) ** {{ill|Mizuhanome|lt=Mizuhanome no Mikoto|ja|弥都波迺売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Kuni no Mikumari no Kami|ja|国水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}} ** {{ill|Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseribime no Mikoto|ja|須勢理比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suseri-Hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== History == === Foundation === According to tradition, the shrine was founded in the 10th year of Emperor Sujin's reign (88 BCE). A giant serpent frequently appeared in the area, leading the emperor to identify it as the aramitama of Ōkuninushi. He dispatched {{ill|Izumo no Furune|ja|出雲振根|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Furune|qq=}} to enshrine the deity, borrowing sacred objects from {{ill|Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo Taisha|ja|杵築大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Taisha|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Place Name Dictionary|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1986}}</ref>
In Emperor Sujin's 62nd year, an oracle declared that "Mikukurumitama" should be the deity's name, and the shrine was established to house this deity alongside four others.
=== Historical Records === The shrine may correspond to the "Kawachi Province Wani Shrine" mentioned in 850 CE, where the deity's Rank was raised to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. During the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, it also served as a clan shrine for the {{ill|Kusunoki family|ja|楠木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusunoki family|1=Kusunoki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541208}}, who venerated the shrine as their ancestral deity.
The shrine was burned down during the {{ill|Siege of Akasaka|en|Siege of Akasaka|WD=Q7509791|lt=Siege of Akasaka}} in 1331 but was later rebuilt under the support of {{ill|Kusunoki Masashige|en|Kusunoki Masashige|WD=Q382234|lt=Kusunoki Masashige}}. In 1585, it was again destroyed during the {{ill|Kishū Campaign|ja|紀州征伐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishū Campaign|1=Battle of Kishuu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10855721}} by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}. The current main shrine was reconstructed in 1660.
The shrine was designated as a gōsha in 1872 during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period}}.
== Shrine Grounds == The shrine grounds include the following structures: * '''Main Shrine (Honden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Upper Worship Hall (Kami-haiden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Lower Worship Hall (Shimo-haiden):''' * '''Treasure Hall (Saigu-kura):''' * '''Gates and Torii:''' * '''Miyajinja Uramayama Kofun Group:''' Includes a keyhole-shaped kofun (58 m long) believed to be from the early {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Uramayama Kofun Explanation Board|publisher=Educational Board of Tondabayashi|accessdate=2020-08-28}}</ref>
== Subsidiary Shrines == * {{ill|Kumano Takahira Shrine|ja|熊野貴平神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Takahira Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Nagi Shrine|ja|南木神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagi Shrine|1=Nagi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22119792}} – Dedicated to the Kusunoki family. * {{ill|Kuni no Ichi Shrine|ja|皇大神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Ichi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神明神社|1=Shinmei shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590310}}
== Annual Festivals == The shrine's annual festival is held on the third Friday of October.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuta Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Mikumari Shrine|qq=}}
== External Links == * [https://osaka-info.jp/page/migukurumitamajinja Migukurumitama Shrine | OSAKA-INFO]

<!--interwikis from wikidata--> <!--interwikis from wikidata--> <!--interwikis from wikidata--> {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Migukurumitama jinja shimohaiden.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Tondabayashi|WD=Q490928}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.51797222222222|N|135.60141666666667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{ '''Migukurumitama Shrine''' (美具久留御魂神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Tondabayashi|en|Tondabayashi|WD=Q490928|lt=Tondabayashi}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is an {{ill|historic shrine|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/historic shrine|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. The shrine is also selected as one of the {{ill|100 Green Selections of Osaka|ja|100 Greens of Ōsaka|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Green Selections of Osaka|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/midorikikaku/toshiryokka/hyakusenn.html|title=Osaka 100 Green Selections|publisher=Osaka Prefecture|accessdate=2016-12-23}}</ref> It is also referred to as the "Shimono Suibun Shrine" (下水分社) and, along with {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuta Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Mikumari Shrine|qq=}} (the Kamisuibun Shrine in {{ill|Chihayaakasaka|en|Chihayaakasaka|WD=Q1072574|lt=Chihayaakasaka}}) and {{ill|Nishikori Shrine|ja|錦織神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikori Shrine|qq=}} (in Tondabayashi), it is one of the "Three Mikumari Shrines of {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province}}."
== Enshrined Deities == * '''Primary Deity:''' Migukurumitama no Ōkami (美具久留御魂大神) – The {{ill|Mitama|en|Mitama|WD=Q495568|lt=Aramitama}} (rough spirit) of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. It is believed to represent a water deity, deriving its name from "Mikukurumitama" (Water Swimming Spirit). * '''Additional Deities:''' ** {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Amemikumari no Kami|ja|天水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}} (Heavenly Mikumari Deity) ** {{ill|Mizuhanome|lt=Mizuhanome no Mikoto|ja|弥都波迺売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Kuni no Mikumari no Kami|ja|国水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}} ** {{ill|Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseribime no Mikoto|ja|須勢理比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suseri-Hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== History == === Foundation === According to tradition, the shrine was founded in the 10th year of Emperor Sujin's reign (88 BCE). A giant serpent frequently appeared in the area, leading the emperor to identify it as the aramitama of Ōkuninushi. He dispatched {{ill|Izumo no Furune|ja|出雲振根|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Furune|qq=}} to enshrine the deity, borrowing sacred objects from {{ill|Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo Taisha|ja|杵築大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Taisha|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Place Name Dictionary|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1986}}</ref>
In Emperor Sujin's 62nd year, an oracle declared that "Mikukurumitama" should be the deity's name, and the shrine was established to house this deity alongside four others.
=== Historical Records === The shrine may correspond to the "Kawachi Province Wani Shrine" mentioned in 850 CE, where the deity's Rank was raised to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. During the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, it also served as a clan shrine for the {{ill|Kusunoki family|ja|楠木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusunoki family|1=Kusunoki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541208}}, who venerated the shrine as their ancestral deity.
The shrine was burned down during the {{ill|Siege of Akasaka|en|Siege of Akasaka|WD=Q7509791|lt=Siege of Akasaka}} in 1331 but was later rebuilt under the support of {{ill|Kusunoki Masashige|en|Kusunoki Masashige|WD=Q382234|lt=Kusunoki Masashige}}. In 1585, it was again destroyed during the {{ill|Kishū Campaign|ja|紀州征伐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishū Campaign|1=Battle of Kishuu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10855721}} by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}. The current main shrine was reconstructed in 1660.
The shrine was designated as a gōsha in 1872 during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period}}.
== Shrine Grounds == The shrine grounds include the following structures: * '''Main Shrine (Honden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Upper Worship Hall (Kami-haiden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Lower Worship Hall (Shimo-haiden):''' * '''Treasure Hall (Saigu-kura):''' * '''Gates and Torii:''' * '''Miyajinja Uramayama Kofun Group:''' Includes a keyhole-shaped kofun (58 m long) believed to be from the early {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Uramayama Kofun Explanation Board|publisher=Educational Board of Tondabayashi|accessdate=2020-08-28}}</ref>
== Subsidiary Shrines == * {{ill|Kumano Takahira Shrine|ja|熊野貴平神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Takahira Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Nagi Shrine|ja|南木神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagi Shrine|1=Nagi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22119792}} – Dedicated to the Kusunoki family. * {{ill|Kuni no Ichi Shrine|ja|皇大神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Ichi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神明神社|1=Shinmei shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590310}}
== Annual Festivals == The shrine's annual festival is held on the third Friday of October.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuta Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Mikumari Shrine|qq=}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata--> <!--interwikis from wikidata--> <!--interwikis from wikidata--> {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Migukurumitama jinja shimohaiden.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Tondabayashi|WD=Q490928}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.51797222222222|N|135.60141666666667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{ '''Migukurumitama Shrine''' (美具久留御魂神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Tondabayashi|en|Tondabayashi|WD=Q490928|lt=Tondabayashi}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is an {{ill|historic shrine|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/historic shrine|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. The shrine is also selected as one of the {{ill|100 Green Selections of Osaka|ja|100 Greens of Ōsaka|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Green Selections of Osaka|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/midorikikaku/toshiryokka/hyakusenn.html|title=Osaka 100 Green Selections|publisher=Osaka Prefecture|accessdate=2016-12-23}}</ref> It is also referred to as the "Shimono Suibun Shrine" (下水分社) and, along with {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuta Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Mikumari Shrine|qq=}} (the Kamisuibun Shrine in {{ill|Chihayaakasaka|en|Chihayaakasaka|WD=Q1072574|lt=Chihayaakasaka}}) and {{ill|Nishikori Shrine|ja|錦織神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikori Shrine|qq=}} (in Tondabayashi), it is one of the "Three Mikumari Shrines of {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province}}."
== Enshrined Deities == * '''Primary Deity:''' Migukurumitama no Ōkami (美具久留御魂大神) – The {{ill|Mitama|en|Mitama|WD=Q495568|lt=Aramitama}} (rough spirit) of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. It is believed to represent a water deity, deriving its name from "Mikukurumitama" (Water Swimming Spirit). * '''Additional Deities:''' ** {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Amemikumari no Kami|ja|天水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}} (Heavenly Mikumari Deity) ** {{ill|Mizuhanome|lt=Mizuhanome no Mikoto|ja|弥都波迺売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Kuni no Mikumari no Kami|ja|国水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}} ** {{ill|Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseribime no Mikoto|ja|須勢理比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suseri-Hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== History == === Foundation === According to tradition, the shrine was founded in the 10th year of Emperor Sujin's reign (88 BCE). A giant serpent frequently appeared in the area, leading the emperor to identify it as the aramitama of Ōkuninushi. He dispatched {{ill|Izumo no Furune|ja|出雲振根|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Furune|qq=}} to enshrine the deity, borrowing sacred objects from {{ill|Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo Taisha|ja|杵築大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Taisha|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Place Name Dictionary|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1986}}</ref>
In Emperor Sujin's 62nd year, an oracle declared that "Mikukurumitama" should be the deity's name, and the shrine was established to house this deity alongside four others.
=== Historical Records === The shrine may correspond to the "Kawachi Province Wani Shrine" mentioned in 850 CE, where the deity's Rank was raised to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. During the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, it also served as a clan shrine for the {{ill|Kusunoki family|ja|楠木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusunoki family|1=Kusunoki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541208}}, who venerated the shrine as their ancestral deity.
The shrine was burned down during the {{ill|Siege of Akasaka|en|Siege of Akasaka|WD=Q7509791|lt=Siege of Akasaka}} in 1331 but was later rebuilt under the support of {{ill|Kusunoki Masashige|en|Kusunoki Masashige|WD=Q382234|lt=Kusunoki Masashige}}. In 1585, it was again destroyed during the {{ill|Kishū Campaign|ja|紀州征伐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishū Campaign|1=Battle of Kishuu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10855721}} by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}. The current main shrine was reconstructed in 1660.
The shrine was designated as a gōsha in 1872 during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period}}.
== Shrine Grounds == The shrine grounds include the following structures: * '''Main Shrine (Honden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Upper Worship Hall (Kami-haiden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Lower Worship Hall (Shimo-haiden):''' * '''Treasure Hall (Saigu-kura):''' * '''Gates and Torii:''' * '''Miyajinja Uramayama Kofun Group:''' Includes a keyhole-shaped kofun (58 m long) believed to be from the early {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Uramayama Kofun Explanation Board|publisher=Educational Board of Tondabayashi|accessdate=2020-08-28}}</ref>
== Subsidiary Shrines == * {{ill|Kumano Takahira Shrine|ja|熊野貴平神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Takahira Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Nagi Shrine|ja|南木神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagi Shrine|1=Nagi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22119792}} – Dedicated to the Kusunoki family. * {{ill|Kuni no Ichi Shrine|ja|皇大神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Ichi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神明神社|1=Shinmei shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590310}}
== Annual Festivals == The shrine's annual festival is held on the third Friday of October.

== See Also == * {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuta Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Mikumari Shrine|qq=}}

The shrine is also selected as one of the {{ill|100 Green Selections of Osaka|ja|100 Greens of Ōsaka|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Green Selections of Osaka|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/midorikikaku/toshiryokka/hyakusenn.html|title=Osaka 100 Green Selections|publisher=Osaka Prefecture|accessdate=2016-12-23}}</ref> It is also referred to as the "Shimono Suibun Shrine" (下水分社) and, along with {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuta Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Mikumari Shrine|qq=}} (the Kamisuibun Shrine in {{ill|Chihayaakasaka|en|Chihayaakasaka|WD=Q1072574|lt=Chihayaakasaka|1=Chihayaakasaka}}) and {{ill|Nishikori Shrine|ja|錦織神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikori Shrine|qq=}} (in Tondabayashi), it is one of the "Three Mikumari Shrines of {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province|1=Kawachi Province}}."
== Enshrined Deities == * '''Primary Deity:''' Migukurumitama no Ōkami (美具久留御魂大神) – The {{ill|Mitama|en|Mitama|WD=Q495568|lt=Aramitama|1=Mitama}} (rough spirit) of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. It is believed to represent a water deity, deriving its name from "Mikukurumitama" (Water Swimming Spirit). * '''Additional Deities:''' ** {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Amemikumari no Kami|ja|天水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}} (Heavenly Mikumari Deity) ** {{ill|Mizuhanome|lt=Mizuhanome no Mikoto|ja|弥都波迺売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Kuni no Mikumari no Kami|ja|国水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}} ** {{ill|Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseribime no Mikoto|ja|須勢理比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suseri-Hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== History == === Foundation === According to tradition, the shrine was founded in the 10th year of Emperor Sujin's reign (88 BCE). A giant serpent frequently appeared in the area, leading the emperor to identify it as the aramitama of Ōkuninushi. He dispatched {{ill|Izumo no Furune|ja|出雲振根|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Furune|qq=}} to enshrine the deity, borrowing sacred objects from {{ill|Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo Taisha|ja|杵築大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Taisha|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Place Name Dictionary|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1986}}</ref>
In Emperor Sujin's 62nd year, an oracle declared that "Mikukurumitama" should be the deity's name, and the shrine was established to house this deity alongside four others.
=== Historical Records === The shrine may correspond to the "Kawachi Province Wani Shrine" mentioned in 850 CE, where the deity's Rank was raised to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. During the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, it also served as a clan shrine for the {{ill|Kusunoki family|ja|楠木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusunoki family|1=Kusunoki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541208}}, who venerated the shrine as their ancestral deity.
The shrine was burned down during the {{ill|Siege of Akasaka|en|Siege of Akasaka|WD=Q7509791|lt=Siege of Akasaka|1=Siege of Akasaka}} in 1331 but was later rebuilt under the support of {{ill|Kusunoki Masashige|en|Kusunoki Masashige|WD=Q382234|lt=Kusunoki Masashige|1=Kusunoki Masashige}}. In 1585, it was again destroyed during the {{ill|Kishū Campaign|ja|紀州征伐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishū Campaign|1=Battle of Kishuu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10855721}} by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}. The current main shrine was reconstructed in 1660.
The shrine was designated as a gōsha in 1872 during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}}.
== Shrine Grounds == The shrine grounds include the following structures: * '''Main Shrine (Honden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Upper Worship Hall (Kami-haiden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Lower Worship Hall (Shimo-haiden):''' * '''Treasure Hall (Saigu-kura):''' * '''Gates and Torii:''' * '''Miyajinja Uramayama Kofun Group:''' Includes a keyhole-shaped kofun (58 m long) believed to be from the early {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Uramayama Kofun Explanation Board|publisher=Educational Board of Tondabayashi|accessdate=2020-08-28}}</ref>
== Subsidiary Shrines == * {{ill|Kumano Takahira Shrine|ja|熊野貴平神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Takahira Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Nagi Shrine|ja|南木神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagi Shrine|1=Nangi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22119792}} – Dedicated to the Kusunoki family. * {{ill|Kuni no Ichi Shrine|ja|皇大神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Ichi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神明神社|1=Shinmei shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590310}}
== Annual Festivals == The shrine's annual festival is held on the third Friday of October.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuta Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Mikumari Shrine|qq=}}
== External Links == * [https://osaka-info.jp/page/migukurumitamajinja Migukurumitama Shrine | OSAKA-INFO]

<!--interwikis from wikidata--> <!--interwikis from wikidata--> <!--interwikis from wikidata--> {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Migukurumitama jinja shimohaiden.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Tondabayashi|WD=Q490928|1=Tondabayashi}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.51797222222222|N|135.60141666666667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{ '''Migukurumitama Shrine''' (美具久留御魂神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Tondabayashi|en|Tondabayashi|WD=Q490928|lt=Tondabayashi|1=Tondabayashi}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is an {{ill|historic shrine|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/historic shrine|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. The shrine is also selected as one of the {{ill|100 Green Selections of Osaka|ja|100 Greens of Ōsaka|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Green Selections of Osaka|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/midorikikaku/toshiryokka/hyakusenn.html|title=Osaka 100 Green Selections|publisher=Osaka Prefecture|accessdate=2016-12-23}}</ref> It is also referred to as the "Shimono Suibun Shrine" (下水分社) and, along with {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuta Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Mikumari Shrine|qq=}} (the Kamisuibun Shrine in {{ill|Chihayaakasaka|en|Chihayaakasaka|WD=Q1072574|lt=Chihayaakasaka|1=Chihayaakasaka}}) and {{ill|Nishikori Shrine|ja|錦織神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikori Shrine|qq=}} (in Tondabayashi), it is one of the "Three Mikumari Shrines of {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province|1=Kawachi Province}}."
== Enshrined Deities == * '''Primary Deity:''' Migukurumitama no Ōkami (美具久留御魂大神) – The {{ill|Mitama|en|Mitama|WD=Q495568|lt=Aramitama|1=Mitama}} (rough spirit) of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. It is believed to represent a water deity, deriving its name from "Mikukurumitama" (Water Swimming Spirit). * '''Additional Deities:''' ** {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Amemikumari no Kami|ja|天水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}} (Heavenly Mikumari Deity) ** {{ill|Mizuhanome|lt=Mizuhanome no Mikoto|ja|弥都波迺売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Kuni no Mikumari no Kami|ja|国水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}} ** {{ill|Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseribime no Mikoto|ja|須勢理比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suseri-Hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== History == === Foundation === According to tradition, the shrine was founded in the 10th year of Emperor Sujin's reign (88 BCE). A giant serpent frequently appeared in the area, leading the emperor to identify it as the aramitama of Ōkuninushi. He dispatched {{ill|Izumo no Furune|ja|出雲振根|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Furune|qq=}} to enshrine the deity, borrowing sacred objects from {{ill|Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo Taisha|ja|杵築大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Taisha|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Place Name Dictionary|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1986}}</ref>
In Emperor Sujin's 62nd year, an oracle declared that "Mikukurumitama" should be the deity's name, and the shrine was established to house this deity alongside four others.
=== Historical Records === The shrine may correspond to the "Kawachi Province Wani Shrine" mentioned in 850 CE, where the deity's Rank was raised to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. During the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, it also served as a clan shrine for the {{ill|Kusunoki family|ja|楠木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusunoki family|1=Kusunoki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541208}}, who venerated the shrine as their ancestral deity.
The shrine was burned down during the {{ill|Siege of Akasaka|en|Siege of Akasaka|WD=Q7509791|lt=Siege of Akasaka|1=Siege of Akasaka}} in 1331 but was later rebuilt under the support of {{ill|Kusunoki Masashige|en|Kusunoki Masashige|WD=Q382234|lt=Kusunoki Masashige|1=Kusunoki Masashige}}. In 1585, it was again destroyed during the {{ill|Kishū Campaign|ja|紀州征伐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishū Campaign|1=Battle of Kishuu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10855721}} by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}. The current main shrine was reconstructed in 1660.
The shrine was designated as a gōsha in 1872 during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}}.
== Shrine Grounds == The shrine grounds include the following structures: * '''Main Shrine (Honden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Upper Worship Hall (Kami-haiden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Lower Worship Hall (Shimo-haiden):''' * '''Treasure Hall (Saigu-kura):''' * '''Gates and Torii:''' * '''Miyajinja Uramayama Kofun Group:''' Includes a keyhole-shaped kofun (58 m long) believed to be from the early {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Uramayama Kofun Explanation Board|publisher=Educational Board of Tondabayashi|accessdate=2020-08-28}}</ref>
== Subsidiary Shrines == * {{ill|Kumano Takahira Shrine|ja|熊野貴平神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Takahira Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Nagi Shrine|ja|南木神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagi Shrine|1=Nangi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22119792}} – Dedicated to the Kusunoki family. * {{ill|Kuni no Ichi Shrine|ja|皇大神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Ichi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神明神社|1=Shinmei shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590310}}
== Annual Festivals == The shrine's annual festival is held on the third Friday of October.

It is believed to represent a water deity, deriving its name from "Mikukurumitama" (Water Swimming Spirit). * '''Additional Deities:''' ** {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Amemikumari no Kami|ja|天水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}} (Heavenly Mikumari Deity) ** {{ill|Mizuhanome|lt=Mizuhanome no Mikoto|ja|弥都波迺売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Kuni no Mikumari no Kami|ja|国水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}} ** {{ill|Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseribime no Mikoto|ja|須勢理比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suseri-Hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== History == === Foundation === According to tradition, the shrine was founded in the 10th year of Emperor Sujin's reign (88 BCE). A giant serpent frequently appeared in the area, leading the emperor to identify it as the aramitama of Ōkuninushi. He dispatched {{ill|Izumo no Furune|ja|出雲振根|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Furune|qq=}} to enshrine the deity, borrowing sacred objects from {{ill|Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo Taisha|ja|杵築大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Taisha|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Place Name Dictionary|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1986}}</ref>
In Emperor Sujin's 62nd year, an oracle declared that "Mikukurumitama" should be the deity's name, and the shrine was established to house this deity alongside four others.
=== Historical Records === The shrine may correspond to the "Kawachi Province Wani Shrine" mentioned in 850 CE, where the deity's Rank was raised to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. During the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, it also served as a clan shrine for the {{ill|Kusunoki family|ja|楠木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusunoki family|1=Kusunoki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541208}}, who venerated the shrine as their ancestral deity.
The shrine was burned down during the {{ill|Siege of Akasaka|en|Siege of Akasaka|WD=Q7509791|lt=Siege of Akasaka|1=Siege of Akasaka}} in 1331 but was later rebuilt under the support of {{ill|Kusunoki Masashige|en|Kusunoki Masashige|WD=Q382234|lt=Kusunoki Masashige|1=Kusunoki Masashige}}. In 1585, it was again destroyed during the {{ill|Kishū Campaign|ja|紀州征伐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishū Campaign|1=Battle of Kishuu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10855721}} by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}. The current main shrine was reconstructed in 1660.
The shrine was designated as a gōsha in 1872 during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}}.
== Shrine Grounds == The shrine grounds include the following structures: * '''Main Shrine (Honden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Upper Worship Hall (Kami-haiden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Lower Worship Hall (Shimo-haiden):''' * '''Treasure Hall (Saigu-kura):''' * '''Gates and Torii:''' * '''Miyajinja Uramayama Kofun Group:''' Includes a keyhole-shaped kofun (58 m long) believed to be from the early {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Uramayama Kofun Explanation Board|publisher=Educational Board of Tondabayashi|accessdate=2020-08-28}}</ref>
== Subsidiary Shrines == * {{ill|Kumano Takahira Shrine|ja|熊野貴平神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Takahira Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Nagi Shrine|ja|南木神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagi Shrine|1=Nangi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22119792}} – Dedicated to the Kusunoki family. * {{ill|Kuni no Ichi Shrine|ja|皇大神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Ichi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神明神社|1=Shinmei shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590310}}
== Annual Festivals == The shrine's annual festival is held on the third Friday of October.

<!--interwikis from wikidata--> <!--interwikis from wikidata--> <!--interwikis from wikidata--> {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Migukurumitama jinja shimohaiden.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Tondabayashi|WD=Q490928|1=Tondabayashi}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.51797222222222|N|135.60141666666667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{ '''Migukurumitama Shrine''' (美具久留御魂神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Tondabayashi|en|Tondabayashi|WD=Q490928|lt=Tondabayashi|1=Tondabayashi}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is an {{ill|historic shrine|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/historic shrine|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. The shrine is also selected as one of the {{ill|100 Green Selections of Osaka|ja|100 Greens of Ōsaka|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Green Selections of Osaka|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/midorikikaku/toshiryokka/hyakusenn.html|title=Osaka 100 Green Selections|publisher=Osaka Prefecture|accessdate=2016-12-23}}</ref> It is also referred to as the "Shimono Suibun Shrine" (下水分社) and, along with {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuta Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Mikumari Shrine|qq=}} (the Kamisuibun Shrine in {{ill|Chihayaakasaka|en|Chihayaakasaka|WD=Q1072574|lt=Chihayaakasaka|1=Chihayaakasaka}}) and {{ill|Nishikori Shrine|ja|錦織神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikori Shrine|qq=}} (in Tondabayashi), it is one of the "Three Mikumari Shrines of {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province|1=Kawachi Province}}."
== Enshrined Deities == * '''Primary Deity:''' Migukurumitama no Ōkami (美具久留御魂大神) – The {{ill|Mitama|en|Mitama|WD=Q495568|lt=Aramitama|1=Mitama}} (rough spirit) of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. It is believed to represent a water deity, deriving its name from "Mikukurumitama" (Water Swimming Spirit). * '''Additional Deities:''' ** {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Amemikumari no Kami|ja|天水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}} (Heavenly Mikumari Deity) ** {{ill|Mizuhanome|lt=Mizuhanome no Mikoto|ja|弥都波迺売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Kuni no Mikumari no Kami|ja|国水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}} ** {{ill|Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseribime no Mikoto|ja|須勢理比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suseri-Hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

== Subsidiary Shrines == * {{ill|Kumano Takahira Shrine|ja|熊野貴平神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Takahira Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Nagi Shrine|ja|南木神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagi Shrine|1=Nangi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22119792}} – Dedicated to the Kusunoki family. * {{ill|Kuni no Ichi Shrine|ja|皇大神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Ichi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神明神社|1=Shinmei shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590310}}
== Annual Festivals == The shrine's annual festival is held on the third Friday of October.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuta Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Mikumari Shrine|qq=}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata--> <!--interwikis from wikidata--> <!--interwikis from wikidata--> {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Migukurumitama jinja shimohaiden.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Tondabayashi|WD=Q490928|1=Tondabayashi}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.51797222222222|N|135.60141666666667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{ '''Migukurumitama Shrine''' (美具久留御魂神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Tondabayashi|en|Tondabayashi|WD=Q490928|lt=Tondabayashi|1=Tondabayashi}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is an {{ill|historic shrine|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/historic shrine|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. The shrine is also selected as one of the {{ill|100 Green Selections of Osaka|ja|100 Greens of Ōsaka|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Green Selections of Osaka|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/midorikikaku/toshiryokka/hyakusenn.html|title=Osaka 100 Green Selections|publisher=Osaka Prefecture|accessdate=2016-12-23}}</ref> It is also referred to as the "Shimono Suibun Shrine" (下水分社) and, along with {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuta Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Mikumari Shrine|qq=}} (the Kamisuibun Shrine in {{ill|Chihayaakasaka|en|Chihayaakasaka|WD=Q1072574|lt=Chihayaakasaka|1=Chihayaakasaka}}) and {{ill|Nishikori Shrine|ja|錦織神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikori Shrine|qq=}} (in Tondabayashi), it is one of the "Three Mikumari Shrines of {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province|1=Kawachi Province}}."
== Enshrined Deities == * '''Primary Deity:''' Migukurumitama no Ōkami (美具久留御魂大神) – The {{ill|Mitama|en|Mitama|WD=Q495568|lt=Aramitama|1=Mitama}} (rough spirit) of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. It is believed to represent a water deity, deriving its name from "Mikukurumitama" (Water Swimming Spirit). * '''Additional Deities:''' ** {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Amemikumari no Kami|ja|天水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}} (Heavenly Mikumari Deity) ** {{ill|Mizuhanome|lt=Mizuhanome no Mikoto|ja|弥都波迺売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Kuni no Mikumari no Kami|ja|国水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}} ** {{ill|Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseribime no Mikoto|ja|須勢理比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suseri-Hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== History == === Foundation === According to tradition, the shrine was founded in the 10th year of Emperor Sujin's reign (88 BCE). A giant serpent frequently appeared in the area, leading the emperor to identify it as the aramitama of Ōkuninushi. He dispatched {{ill|Izumo no Furune|ja|出雲振根|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Furune|qq=}} to enshrine the deity, borrowing sacred objects from {{ill|Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo Taisha|ja|杵築大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Taisha|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Place Name Dictionary|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1986}}</ref>
In Emperor Sujin's 62nd year, an oracle declared that "Mikukurumitama" should be the deity's name, and the shrine was established to house this deity alongside four others.
=== Historical Records === The shrine may correspond to the "Kawachi Province Wani Shrine" mentioned in 850 CE, where the deity's Rank was raised to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. During the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, it also served as a clan shrine for the {{ill|Kusunoki family|ja|楠木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusunoki family|1=Kusunoki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541208}}, who venerated the shrine as their ancestral deity.
The shrine was burned down during the {{ill|Siege of Akasaka|en|Siege of Akasaka|WD=Q7509791|lt=Siege of Akasaka|1=Siege of Akasaka}} in 1331 but was later rebuilt under the support of {{ill|Kusunoki Masashige|en|Kusunoki Masashige|WD=Q382234|lt=Kusunoki Masashige|1=Kusunoki Masashige}}. In 1585, it was again destroyed during the {{ill|Kishū Campaign|ja|紀州征伐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishū Campaign|1=Battle of Kishuu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10855721}} by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}. The current main shrine was reconstructed in 1660.
The shrine was designated as a gōsha in 1872 during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}}.
== Shrine Grounds == The shrine grounds include the following structures: * '''Main Shrine (Honden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Upper Worship Hall (Kami-haiden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Lower Worship Hall (Shimo-haiden):''' * '''Treasure Hall (Saigu-kura):''' * '''Gates and Torii:''' * '''Miyajinja Uramayama Kofun Group:''' Includes a keyhole-shaped kofun (58 m long) believed to be from the early {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Uramayama Kofun Explanation Board|publisher=Educational Board of Tondabayashi|accessdate=2020-08-28}}</ref>
== Subsidiary Shrines == * {{ill|Kumano Takahira Shrine|ja|熊野貴平神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Takahira Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Nagi Shrine|ja|南木神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagi Shrine|1=Nangi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22119792}} – Dedicated to the Kusunoki family. * {{ill|Kuni no Ichi Shrine|ja|皇大神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Ichi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神明神社|1=Shinmei shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590310}}
== Annual Festivals == The shrine's annual festival is held on the third Friday of October.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuta Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Mikumari Shrine|qq=}}
== External Links == * [https://osaka-info.jp/page/migukurumitamajinja Migukurumitama Shrine | OSAKA-INFO]
{{Munakata Faith}}

'''Migukurumitama Shrine (Q11608777)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].
== Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | RONSHA || {{ill|Migukurumitama Shrine|lt=Migukurumitama Shrine|ja|美具久留御魂神社|commons|Category:Migukurumitama-jinja|WD=Q11608777|1=Migukurumitama Shrine}} || {{ill|Kanpei-sha|WD=Q135160338|lt=Kanpei|comment=categorized|1=Kanpei-sha}} || — || — || — || {{coord|34.517986|135.601478|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=180880 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q11608777}}==Notes== <references group="lower-alpha"/> [[co:commons:Category:Migukurumitama-jinja]] [[da:d:Q11608777]] [[ja:美具久留御魂神社]] [[Category:sn_crud|commons:Category:Migukurumitama-jinja]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tondabayashi, Osaka]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tondabayashi, Osaka]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata--> <!--interwikis from wikidata--> <!--interwikis from wikidata--> {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Migukurumitama jinja shimohaiden.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Tondabayashi|WD=Q490928|1=Tondabayashi}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.51797222222222|N|135.60141666666667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{ '''Migukurumitama Shrine''' (美具久留御魂神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Tondabayashi|en|Tondabayashi|WD=Q490928|lt=Tondabayashi|1=Tondabayashi}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is an {{ill|historic shrine|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/historic shrine|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. The shrine is also selected as one of the {{ill|100 Green Selections of Osaka|ja|100 Greens of Ōsaka|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Green Selections of Osaka|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/midorikikaku/toshiryokka/hyakusenn.html|title=Osaka 100 Green Selections|publisher=Osaka Prefecture|accessdate=2016-12-23}}</ref> It is also referred to as the "Shimono Suibun Shrine" (下水分社) and, along with {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuta Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Mikumari Shrine|qq=}} (the Kamisuibun Shrine in {{ill|Chihayaakasaka|en|Chihayaakasaka|WD=Q1072574|lt=Chihayaakasaka|1=Chihayaakasaka}}) and {{ill|Nishikori Shrine|ja|錦織神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikori Shrine|qq=}} (in Tondabayashi), it is one of the "Three Mikumari Shrines of {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province|1=Kawachi Province}}."
== Enshrined Deities == * '''Primary Deity:''' Migukurumitama no Ōkami (美具久留御魂大神) – The {{ill|Mitama|en|Mitama|WD=Q495568|lt=Aramitama|1=Mitama}} (rough spirit) of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. It is believed to represent a water deity, deriving its name from "Mikukurumitama" (Water Swimming Spirit). * '''Additional Deities:''' ** {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Amemikumari no Kami|ja|天水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}} (Heavenly Mikumari Deity) ** {{ill|Mizuhanome|lt=Mizuhanome no Mikoto|ja|弥都波迺売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Kuni no Mikumari no Kami|ja|国水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}} ** {{ill|Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseribime no Mikoto|ja|須勢理比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suseri-Hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== History == === Foundation === According to tradition, the shrine was founded in the 10th year of Emperor Sujin's reign (88 BCE). A giant serpent frequently appeared in the area, leading the emperor to identify it as the aramitama of Ōkuninushi. He dispatched {{ill|Izumo no Furune|ja|出雲振根|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Furune|qq=}} to enshrine the deity, borrowing sacred objects from {{ill|Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo Taisha|ja|杵築大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Taisha|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Place Name Dictionary|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1986}}</ref>
In Emperor Sujin's 62nd year, an oracle declared that "Mikukurumitama" should be the deity's name, and the shrine was established to house this deity alongside four others.
=== Historical Records === The shrine may correspond to the "Kawachi Province Wani Shrine" mentioned in 850 CE, where the deity's Rank was raised to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. During the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, it also served as a clan shrine for the {{ill|Kusunoki family|ja|楠木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusunoki family|1=Kusunoki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541208}}, who venerated the shrine as their ancestral deity.
The shrine was burned down during the {{ill|Siege of Akasaka|en|Siege of Akasaka|WD=Q7509791|lt=Siege of Akasaka|1=Siege of Akasaka}} in 1331 but was later rebuilt under the support of {{ill|Kusunoki Masashige|en|Kusunoki Masashige|WD=Q382234|lt=Kusunoki Masashige|1=Kusunoki Masashige}}. In 1585, it was again destroyed during the {{ill|Kishū Campaign|ja|紀州征伐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishū Campaign|1=Battle of Kishuu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10855721}} by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}. The current main shrine was reconstructed in 1660.
The shrine was designated as a gōsha in 1872 during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}}.
== Shrine Grounds == The shrine grounds include the following structures: * '''Main Shrine (Honden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Upper Worship Hall (Kami-haiden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Lower Worship Hall (Shimo-haiden):''' * '''Treasure Hall (Saigu-kura):''' * '''Gates and Torii:''' * '''Miyajinja Uramayama Kofun Group:''' Includes a keyhole-shaped kofun (58 m long) believed to be from the early {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Uramayama Kofun Explanation Board|publisher=Educational Board of Tondabayashi|accessdate=2020-08-28}}</ref>
== Subsidiary Shrines == * {{ill|Kumano Takahira Shrine|ja|熊野貴平神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Takahira Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Nagi Shrine|ja|南木神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagi Shrine|1=Nangi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22119792}} – Dedicated to the Kusunoki family. * {{ill|Kuni no Ichi Shrine|ja|皇大神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Ichi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神明神社|1=Shinmei shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590310}}
== Annual Festivals == The shrine's annual festival is held on the third Friday of October.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuta Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Mikumari Shrine|qq=}}
== External Links == * [https://osaka-info.jp/page/migukurumitamajinja Migukurumitama Shrine | OSAKA-INFO]

<!--interwikis from wikidata--> <!--interwikis from wikidata--> <!--interwikis from wikidata--> {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Migukurumitama jinja shimohaiden.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Tondabayashi|qid=Q490928|1=Tondabayashi}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.51797222222222|N|135.60141666666667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{ '''Migukurumitama Shrine''' (美具久留御魂神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Tondabayashi|en|Tondabayashi|qid=Q490928|lt=Tondabayashi|1=Tondabayashi}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is an {{ill|historic shrine|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/historic shrine|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. The shrine is also selected as one of the {{ill|100 Green Selections of Osaka|ja|100 Greens of Ōsaka|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Green Selections of Osaka|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/midorikikaku/toshiryokka/hyakusenn.html|title=Osaka 100 Green Selections|publisher=Osaka Prefecture|accessdate=2016-12-23}}</ref> It is also referred to as the "Shimono Suibun Shrine" (下水分社) and, along with {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuta Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Mikumari Shrine|qq=}} (the Kamisuibun Shrine in {{ill|Chihayaakasaka|en|Chihayaakasaka|qid=Q1072574|lt=Chihayaakasaka|1=Chihayaakasaka}}) and {{ill|Nishikori Shrine|ja|錦織神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikori Shrine|qq=}} (in Tondabayashi), it is one of the "Three Mikumari Shrines of {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|qid=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province|1=Kawachi Province}}."
== Enshrined Deities == * '''Primary Deity:''' Migukurumitama no Ōkami (美具久留御魂大神) – The {{ill|Mitama|en|Mitama|qid=Q495568|lt=Aramitama|1=Mitama}} (rough spirit) of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. It is believed to represent a water deity, deriving its name from "Mikukurumitama" (Water Swimming Spirit). * '''Additional Deities:''' ** {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Amemikumari no Kami|ja|天水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}} (Heavenly Mikumari Deity) ** {{ill|Mizuhanome|lt=Mizuhanome no Mikoto|ja|弥都波迺売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Kuni no Mikumari no Kami|ja|国水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}} ** {{ill|Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseribime no Mikoto|ja|須勢理比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suseri-Hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== History == === Foundation === According to tradition, the shrine was founded in the 10th year of Emperor Sujin's reign (88 BCE). A giant serpent frequently appeared in the area, leading the emperor to identify it as the aramitama of Ōkuninushi. He dispatched {{ill|Izumo no Furune|ja|出雲振根|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Furune|qq=}} to enshrine the deity, borrowing sacred objects from {{ill|Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo Taisha|ja|杵築大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Taisha|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Place Name Dictionary|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1986}}</ref>
In Emperor Sujin's 62nd year, an oracle declared that "Mikukurumitama" should be the deity's name, and the shrine was established to house this deity alongside four others.
=== Historical Records === The shrine may correspond to the "Kawachi Province Wani Shrine" mentioned in 850 CE, where the deity's Rank was raised to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. During the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, it also served as a clan shrine for the {{ill|Kusunoki family|ja|楠木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusunoki family|1=Kusunoki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11541208}}, who venerated the shrine as their ancestral deity.
The shrine was burned down during the {{ill|Siege of Akasaka|en|Siege of Akasaka|qid=Q7509791|lt=Siege of Akasaka|1=Siege of Akasaka}} in 1331 but was later rebuilt under the support of {{ill|Kusunoki Masashige|en|Kusunoki Masashige|qid=Q382234|lt=Kusunoki Masashige|1=Kusunoki Masashige}}. In 1585, it was again destroyed during the {{ill|Kishū Campaign|ja|紀州征伐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishū Campaign|1=Battle of Kishuu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10855721}} by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qid=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}. The current main shrine was reconstructed in 1660.
The shrine was designated as a gōsha in 1872 during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}}.
== Shrine Grounds == The shrine grounds include the following structures: * '''Main Shrine (Honden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Upper Worship Hall (Kami-haiden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Lower Worship Hall (Shimo-haiden):''' * '''Treasure Hall (Saigu-kura):''' * '''Gates and Torii:''' * '''Miyajinja Uramayama Kofun Group:''' Includes a keyhole-shaped kofun (58 m long) believed to be from the early {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Uramayama Kofun Explanation Board|publisher=Educational Board of Tondabayashi|accessdate=2020-08-28}}</ref>
== Subsidiary Shrines == * {{ill|Kumano Takahira Shrine|ja|熊野貴平神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Takahira Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Nagi Shrine|ja|南木神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagi Shrine|1=Nangi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q22119792}} – Dedicated to the Kusunoki family. * {{ill|Kuni no Ichi Shrine|ja|皇大神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Ichi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神明神社|1=Shinmei shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11590310}}
== Annual Festivals == The shrine's annual festival is held on the third Friday of October.

<!--interwikis from wikidata--> <!--interwikis from wikidata--> <!--interwikis from wikidata--> {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Migukurumitama jinja shimohaiden.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Tondabayashi|qid=Q490928|1=Tondabayashi}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.51797222222222|N|135.60141666666667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{ '''Migukurumitama Shrine''' (美具久留御魂神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Tondabayashi|en|Tondabayashi|qid=Q490928|lt=Tondabayashi|1=Tondabayashi}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is an {{ill|historic shrine|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/historic shrine|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was formerly Ranked as a {{ill|gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. The shrine is also selected as one of the {{ill|100 Green Selections of Osaka|ja|100 Greens of Ōsaka|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Green Selections of Osaka|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/midorikikaku/toshiryokka/hyakusenn.html|title=Osaka 100 Green Selections|publisher=Osaka Prefecture|accessdate=2016-12-23}}</ref> It is also referred to as the "Shimono Suibun Shrine" (下水分社) and, along with {{ill|Tatsuta Shrine|ja|建水分神社|1=Take Mikumari Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuta Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Mikumari Shrine|qq=}} (the Kamisuibun Shrine in {{ill|Chihayaakasaka|en|Chihayaakasaka|qid=Q1072574|lt=Chihayaakasaka|1=Chihayaakasaka}}) and {{ill|Nishikori Shrine|ja|錦織神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikori Shrine|qq=}} (in Tondabayashi), it is one of the "Three Mikumari Shrines of {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|qid=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province|1=Kawachi Province}}."
== Enshrined Deities == * '''Primary Deity:''' Migukurumitama no Ōkami (美具久留御魂大神) – The {{ill|Mitama|en|Mitama|qid=Q495568|lt=Aramitama|1=Mitama}} (rough spirit) of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. It is believed to represent a water deity, deriving its name from "Mikukurumitama" (Water Swimming Spirit). * '''Additional Deities:''' ** {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Amemikumari no Kami|ja|天水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}} (Heavenly Mikumari Deity) ** {{ill|Mizuhanome|lt=Mizuhanome no Mikoto|ja|弥都波迺売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** {{ill|Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|lt=Kuni no Mikumari no Kami|ja|国水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}} ** {{ill|Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseribime no Mikoto|ja|須勢理比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suseri-Hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== History == === Foundation === According to tradition, the shrine was founded in the 10th year of Emperor Sujin's reign (88 BCE). A giant serpent frequently appeared in the area, leading the emperor to identify it as the aramitama of Ōkuninushi. He dispatched {{ill|Izumo no Furune|ja|出雲振根|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Furune|qq=}} to enshrine the deity, borrowing sacred objects from {{ill|Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo Taisha|ja|杵築大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Taisha|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Japanese Place Name Dictionary|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1986}}</ref>
In Emperor Sujin's 62nd year, an oracle declared that "Mikukurumitama" should be the deity's name, and the shrine was established to house this deity alongside four others.
=== Historical Records === The shrine may correspond to the "Kawachi Province Wani Shrine" mentioned in 850 CE, where the deity's Rank was raised to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. During the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, it also served as a clan shrine for the {{ill|Kusunoki family|ja|楠木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusunoki family|1=Kusunoki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11541208}}, who venerated the shrine as their ancestral deity.
The shrine was burned down during the {{ill|Siege of Akasaka|en|Siege of Akasaka|qid=Q7509791|lt=Siege of Akasaka|1=Siege of Akasaka}} in 1331 but was later rebuilt under the support of {{ill|Kusunoki Masashige|en|Kusunoki Masashige|qid=Q382234|lt=Kusunoki Masashige|1=Kusunoki Masashige}}. In 1585, it was again destroyed during the {{ill|Kishū Campaign|ja|紀州征伐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishū Campaign|1=Battle of Kishuu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10855721}} by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qid=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}. The current main shrine was reconstructed in 1660.
The shrine was designated as a gōsha in 1872 during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}}.
== Shrine Grounds == The shrine grounds include the following structures: * '''Main Shrine (Honden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Upper Worship Hall (Kami-haiden):''' Rebuilt in 1660. * '''Lower Worship Hall (Shimo-haiden):''' * '''Treasure Hall (Saigu-kura):''' * '''Gates and Torii:''' * '''Miyajinja Uramayama Kofun Group:''' Includes a keyhole-shaped kofun (58 m long) believed to be from the early {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Uramayama Kofun Explanation Board|publisher=Educational Board of Tondabayashi|accessdate=2020-08-28}}</ref>
== Subsidiary Shrines == * {{ill|Kumano Takahira Shrine|ja|熊野貴平神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Takahira Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Nagi Shrine|ja|南木神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagi Shrine|1=Nangi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q22119792}} – Dedicated to the Kusunoki family. * {{ill|Kuni no Ichi Shrine|ja|皇大神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Ichi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神明神社|1=Shinmei shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11590310}}
== Annual Festivals == The shrine's annual festival is held on the third Friday of October.
